Bottled water may be carbonated or not with packaging sizes ranging from small single-serving bottles to large carboys for water coolers.

objects.title: Mongoose
objects.description: Family of mammals in Africa and Asia
objects.extract: A mongoose is a small terrestrial carnivorous mammal belonging to the family Herpestidae. This family has two subfamilies, the Herpestinae and the Mungotinae.

objects.title: Corn dolly
objects.extract:
Corn dollies or corn mothers are a form of straw work made as part of harvest customs of Europe before mechanisation.
Scholars of the 18th and 19th centuries theorized that before Christianisation, in traditional pagan European culture it was believed that the spirit of the corn (in American English, "corn" would be "grain") lived amongst the crop, and that

objects.title: Stadt Rapperswil
objects.extract: The Paddle steamship Stadt Rapperswil is the younger of the two remaining steam paddle ships on Lake Zürich. Stadt Rapperswil was built in 1914 by Escher, Wyss & Cie.

objects.title: Pliers
objects.extract: Pliers are a hand tool used to hold objects firmly, possibly developed from tongs used to handle hot metal in Bronze Age Europe. They are also useful for bending and physically compressing a wide range of materials.

objects.title: Goose
objects.description: Common name for a group of waterfowl
objects.extract: A goose (pl.: geese) is a bird of any of several waterfowl species in the family Anatidae. This group comprises the genera Anser (grey geese and white geese) and Branta (black geese).

objects.title: List of pasta
objects.extract: There are many different varieties of pasta. They are usually sorted by size, being long (pasta lunga), short (pasta corta), stuffed (ripiena), cooked in broth (pastina), stretched (strascinati) or in dumpling-like form (gnocchi/gnocchetti).

objects.title: Hornet
objects.extract: Hornets are wasps of the genus Vespa in the subfamily Vespinae (the vespine wasps). They are the largest of the eusocial wasps.

objects.title: Rose
objects.extract: A rose is either a woody perennial flowering plant of the genus Rosa (), in the family Rosaceae (), or the flower it bears. There are over three hundred species and tens of thousands of cultivars.

objects.title: kobza
objects.extract: The kobza (Ukrainian: кобза), also called bandura (Ukrainian: бандура), is a Ukrainian folk music instrument of the lute family (Hornbostel–Sachs classification number 321.321-5+6), a relative of the Central European mandora. The term kobza however, has also been applied to a number of other Eastern European instruments distinct from the Ukrainian kobza.

objects.title: Display case
objects.extract: A display case (also called a showcase, display cabinet, shadow box, or vitrine) is a cabinet with one or often more transparent tempered glass (or plastic, normally acrylic for strength) surfaces, used to display objects for viewing. A display case may appear in an exhibition, museum, retail store, restaurant, or house.

objects.title: Foucault gyroscope - CnAM 7688
objects.description: Gyroscope designed by Léon Foucault and built by Pierre Dumoulin-Froment for the Exposition Universelle of 1867

objects.title: Socket 754
objects.description: CPU socket for old AMD CPUs
objects.extract: Socket 754 is a CPU socket originally developed by AMD to supersede its Athlon XP platform (Socket A, also referred to as Socket 462). Socket 754 was one of the first sockets developed by AMD to support their new 64-bit microprocessor family known as AMD64, this time for the consumer market.

objects.title: Snowplow
objects.extract: A snowplow (also snow plow, snowplough or snow plough) is a device intended for mounting on a vehicle, used for removing snow and ice from outdoor surfaces, typically those serving transportation purposes. Although this term is often used to refer to vehicles mounting such devices, more accurately they are known as winter service vehicles, especially in area

objects.title: Rattlesnake
objects.extract: Rattlesnakes are venomous snakes that form the genera Crotalus and Sistrurus of the subfamily Crotalinae (the pit vipers). Rattlesnakes are predators that live in a wide array of habitats, hunting small animals such as birds and rodents.

objects.title: Chocolate coin
objects.description: Gold foil covered chocolates in the shape of coins
objects.extract: Chocolate coins or chocolate money are foil-covered chocolates in the shape of coins. They are usually created with milk chocolate and are traditionally packaged in small mesh bags.

objects.title: Fuel container
objects.extract: A fuel container  is a container such as a steel can, bottle, drum, etc. for transporting, storing, and dispensing various fuels.

objects.title: Mitsui
objects.extract:
Mitsui Group (三井グループ, Mitsui Gurūpu)  is a group of autonomous Japanese multinational companies.

The major companies of the group include Mitsui & Co.

objects.title: Chandelier
objects.extract: A chandelier () is an ornamental lighting device, typically with spreading branched supports for multiple lights, designed to be hung from the ceiling. Chandeliers are often ornate, and they were originally designed to hold candles, but now incandescent light bulbs as well as fluorescent lamps and LEDs are commonly used.

objects.title: HNLMS Christiaan Cornelis
objects.extract: Christiaan Cornelis, initially called K 2, was a K-class torpedo boat of the Dutch Koninklijke Marine. She saw action during World War II, however she was outdated by then and had lost most of her usefulness.

objects.title: Bell
objects.extract: A bell /ˈbɛl/ () is a directly struck idiophone percussion instrument. Most bells have the shape of a hollow cup that—when struck—vibrates in a single strong strike tone, with its sides forming an efficient resonator.

objects.title: Bast shoe
objects.description: Traditional bast fiber footwear of Europe
objects.extract: Bast shoes are shoes made primarily from bast—fiber taken from the bark of trees, such as linden. They are a kind of basket, woven and fitted to the shape of a foot.

objects.title: Kepi
objects.description: Flat circular cap with a visor
objects.extract: The kepi (English:   or ) is a cap with a flat circular top and a peak, or visor. In English, the term is a loanword from French: képi, itself a re-spelled version of the Alemannic German: Käppi, a diminutive form of Kappe, meaning 'cap'.

objects.title: Air conditioning
objects.description: Cooling of air in an enclosed space
objects.extract: Air conditioning, often abbreviated as A/C (US) or air con (UK), is the process of removing heat from an enclosed space to achieve a more comfortable interior temperature and, in some cases, controlling the humidity of internal air. Air conditioning can be achieved using a mechanical 'air conditioner' or through other methods, such as passive cooling and ven

objects.title: Shield
objects.description: Item of armour carried to intercept attacks or projectiles
objects.extract: A shield is a piece of personal armour held in the hand, which may or may not be strapped to the wrist or forearm. Shields are used to intercept specific attacks, whether from close-ranged weaponry like spears or long ranged projectiles such as arrows.

objects.title: Coddingtonia huifengi
objects.artist: Feng C, Lin Y (2019) Three new species of the genus Coddingtonia from Asia (Aran

objects.title: Pansori
objects.extract: Pansori (Korean: 판소리) is a Korean genre of musical storytelling performed by a singer and a drummer. The term pansori is a compounds of the Korean words pan 판 and sori 소리, the latter of which means "sound." However, pan has multiple meanings, and scholars disagree on which was the intended meaning when the term was coined.

objects.title: Squilla deceptrix
objects.artist: FWC Fish and Wildlife Research Institute

objects.title: Sculpture
objects.extract: Sculpture is the branch of the visual arts that operates in three dimensions. Sculpture is the three-dimensional artwork which is physically presented in the dimensions of height, width and depth.

objects.title: Flexible intermediate bulk container
objects.extract:
A flexible intermediate bulk container (FIBC), jumbo bag, bulk bag, dumpy bag, super sack or a big bag is an industrial container made of flexible fabric that is designed for storing and transporting dry, flowable products, such as sand, fertilizer, and granules of plastic.
FIBCs are most often made of thick woven strands of oriented polypropylene, either co

objects.title: Sari
objects.description: Woman's draped garment of the Indian subcontinent
objects.extract: A sari (Hindi: [saːɽiː]; sometimes also saree, sharee, or sadi) is a draped cloth and women's garment in the Indian subcontinent. It consists of an un-stitched stretch of woven fabric arranged over the body as a dress, with one end attached to the waist, while the other end rests over one shoulder as a stole, sometimes baring a part of the midriff.

objects.title: Chinese dragon
objects.extract: The Chinese dragon, known as long or loong (/lʊŋ/ LUUNG) is a legendary creature in Chinese mythology, Chinese folklore, and Chinese culture generally. Chinese dragons have many animal-like forms, such as turtles and fish, but are most commonly depicted as snake-like with four legs.

objects.title: Green-crowned Brilliant
objects.extract: The green-crowned brilliant (Heliodoxa jacula) is species of hummingbird in the "brilliants", tribe Heliantheini in subfamily Lesbiinae. It is found in Colombia, Costa Rica, Ecuador, and Panama.

objects.title: Engraving
objects.description: Incising designs by cutting into a surface
objects.extract: Engraving is the practice of incising a design on a hard, usually flat surface by cutting grooves into it with a burin. The result may be a decorated object in itself, as when silver, gold, steel, or glass are engraved, or may provide an intaglio printing plate, of copper or another metal, for printing images on paper as prints or illustrations; these images

objects.title: Plague doctor costume
objects.description: Clothing worn by plague doctors that was intended to protect them from infection
objects.extract: The clothing worn by plague doctors was intended to protect them from airborne diseases during outbreaks of bubonic plague in Europe. It is often seen as a symbol of death and disease.

objects.title: Ladle
objects.description: ladle at the Metropolitan Museum of Art (MET, 34.100.204)

objects.title: PET bottle recycling
objects.description: Recycling of bottles made of polyethylene terephthalate
objects.extract: Polyethylene terephthalate (PET) is one of the most common polymers in its polyester family. Its global market size was estimated to be worth US$37.25 billion in 2021.

objects.title: Linden Oak
objects.description: Historic oak tree in North Bethesda, Maryland, US
objects.extract:
The Linden Oak was a large white oak tree in North Bethesda, Maryland, beside the junction of Rockville Pike and Rock Creek Park's Beach Drive.
Believed to have been seeded around 1718, the white oak was among the country's oldest.

objects.title: Product placement
objects.extract: Product placement, also known as embedded marketing, is a marketing technique where references to specific brands or products are incorporated into another work, such as a film or television program, with specific promotional intent.  Much of this is done by loaning products, especially when expensive items, such as vehicles, are involved.

objects.title: Rosary
objects.extract: The Rosary (; Latin: rosarium, in the sense of "crown of roses" or "garland of roses"), formally known as the Psalter of Jesus and Mary (Latin: Psalterium Jesu et Mariae), also known as the Dominican Rosary (as distinct from other forms of rosary such as the Franciscan Crown, Bridgettine Rosary, Rosary of the Holy Wounds, etc.), refers to a set of prayers us

objects.title: Heliodor beryl-MCG 79777
objects.description: sample of heliodor beryl, 400-gramme crystal found in Volodarsk, Ukraine

objects.title: Sari
objects.description: Woman's draped garment of the Indian subcontinent
objects.extract: A sari (Hindi: [saːɽiː]; sometimes also saree, sharee, or sadi) is a draped cloth and women's garment in the Indian subcontinent. It consists of an un-stitched stretch of woven fabric arranged over the body as a dress, with one end attached to the waist, while the other end rests over one shoulder as a stole, sometimes baring a part of the midriff.

objects.title: Adjustable spanner
objects.description: Wrench that can be adjusted to handle various sizes of fasteners
objects.extract: An adjustable spanner (UK), also called a shifting spanner or shifter (Australia and New Zealand) or adjustable wrench (US and Canada), is any of various styles of spanner (wrench) with a movable jaw, allowing it to be used with different sizes of fastener head (nut, bolt, etc.) rather than just one fastener size, as with a conventional fixed spanner.

objects.title: Piaggio Free
objects.extract: The Piaggio Free is a scooter produced by italian motorcycle manufacturer Piaggio from 1992 to 2002 in Pontedera.

objects.title: Mobile phone
objects.description: Portable device to make telephone calls using a radio link
objects.extract: A mobile phone or cell phone is a portable wireless  telephone that allows users to make and receive calls over a radio frequency link while moving within a designated telephone service area, unlike fixed-location phones (landline phones). This radio frequency link connects to the switching systems of a mobile phone operator, providing access to the public s

objects.title: Bamboo
objects.description: Subfamily of plants in the grass family
objects.extract: Bamboos are a diverse group of mostly evergreen perennial flowering plants making up the subfamily Bambusoideae of the grass family Poaceae. Giant bamboos are the largest members of the grass family, in the case of Dendrocalamus sinicus having individual stalks (culms) reaching a length of 46 meters (151 ft), up to 36 centimeters (14 in) in diameter and a we

objects.title: Basketball
objects.extract: Basketball is a team sport in which two teams of five players each (excluding substitutes), opposing one another on a rectangular court, compete with the primary objective of shooting a basketball through a hoop (a basket mounted to a backboard) at each end of the court. Teams alternate between offense, when they attempt to score, and defense, when they try

objects.title: Coral
objects.description: Marine invertebrates of the subphylum Anthozoa
objects.extract: Corals are colonial marine invertebrates within the subphylum Anthozoa of the phylum Cnidaria. They typically form compact colonies of many identical individual polyps.

objects.title: Tartan
objects.extract: Tartan (Scottish Gaelic: breacan [ˈpɾʲɛxkən]), also known, especially in American English, as plaid  (), is a patterned cloth consisting of crossing horizontal and vertical bands in multiple colours, forming repeating symmetrical patterns known as setts. Tartan patterns vary in complexity, from simple two-colour designs to intricate motifs with over twenty h

objects.title: Sidelifter
objects.extract: A sidelifter is a specialised vehicle or semi-trailer used to hoist and transport ISO standard intermodal containers over longer distances.

objects.title: Amateur radio
objects.description: Non-commercial use of the radio spectrum
objects.extract: Amateur radio, also known as Ham radio, is the use of specific bands and frequencies within the radio spectrum for non-commercial communication, technical experimentation, self-training, recreation, radiosport, contesting, and emergency communications. A surge of popular interest in radio experimentation in the early 20th century necessitated that parts of t

objects.title: Pumpkin
objects.description: Category of culinary winter Cucurbita squashes
objects.extract: A pumpkin is a cultivated winter squash in the genus Cucurbita. The term is most commonly applied to round, orange-colored squash varieties, but does not possess a scientific definition.

objects.title: JA84RC
objects.description: DHC-8-400 aircraft with serial number 4550 operated by Ryukyu Air Commuter

objects.title: Hynobius ikioi
objects.extract: Hynobius ikioi is a species of salamander in the family Hynobiidae, endemic to Kyushu in Japan.

objects.title: Fish
objects.artist: Photographes : Éléna Gotsiridzé (Q108170321) et Cynthia Beaudet (Q108170335), po

objects.title: Refrigerator
objects.extract: A refrigerator, commonly shortened to fridge, is a commercial and home appliance consisting of a thermally insulated compartment and a heat pump (mechanical, electronic or chemical) that transfers heat from its inside to its external environment so that its inside is cooled to a temperature below the ambient temperature of the room. Refrigeration is an essen

objects.title: Fragment from Red-Figure Oinochoe (Wine Jug) or Hydria (Water Jar): Woman Holding Box
objects.description: ceramic by Shuvalov Painter (Greek, Attic, active c. 440-410 BC) (1915.533.d)

objects.title: Sushi
objects.extract: Sushi (すし, 寿司, 鮨, 鮓; pronounced [sɯɕiꜜ] or [sɯꜜɕi] ) is a traditional Japanese dish made with vinegared rice (鮨飯, sushi-meshi), typically seasoned with sugar and salt, and combined with a variety of ingredients (ねた, neta), such as seafood, vegetables, or meat; raw seafood is the most common, although some may be cooked. While sushi has numerous styles and pr

objects.title: Sander
objects.extract: A sander is a power tool used to smooth surfaces by abrasion with sandpaper. Sanders have a means to attach the sandpaper and a mechanism to move it rapidly contained within a housing with means to handhold it or fix it to a workbench.

objects.title: Marc Isambard Brunel
objects.extract: Sir Marc Isambard Brunel (, French: [maʁk izɑ̃baʁ bʁynɛl]; 25 April 1769 – 12 December 1849) was a French-British engineer who is most famous for the work he did in Britain. He constructed the Thames Tunnel, the world's first underwater tunnel, and was the father of Isambard Kingdom Brunel.

objects.title: List of Hawaiian dishes
objects.extract: This is a list of dishes in Hawaiian cuisine, which includes Native Hawaiian cuisine and the broader fusion cuisine of Hawaii. The cuisine of Hawaii refers to the indigenous, ethnic, and local cuisines within the diverse state of Hawaii.

objects.title: Covered Tureen in the Form of a Pigeon
Nintendo
objects.extract: Nintendo Co., Ltd. is a Japanese multinational video game company headquartered in Kyoto.

objects.title: Video telematics
objects.description: Technology combining video cameras with telematics data for vehicles and personnel
objects.extract: Video telematics is a technology that combines video cameras with telematics data, such as GPS location and sensor readings, to provide a real-time, contextual view of assets and personnel as part of a tracking or automatic vehicle location solution. According to industry analysis, the adoption of video telematics has been the "hottest industry trend over th

objects.title: Xenopus laevis
objects.extract: The African clawed frog (Xenopus laevis), also known as simply xenopus, African clawed toad, African claw-toed frog or the platanna) is a species of African aquatic frog of the family Pipidae. Its name is derived from the short black claws on its feet.
objects.artist: No machine-readable author provided. Ltshears assumed (based on copyright claims

objects.title: Cholado
objects.extract: Cholado, raspao, or raspado is an icy beverage with fresh fruit and sweetened condensed milk traditionally from Jamundí, in the region of Valle del Cauca, Colombia. It is made from crushed ice or shaved ice, chopped fruit, condensed milk, fruit syrup, and served with a wafer cookie.

objects.title: Traffic light
objects.description: Signalling device to control competing flows of traffic
objects.extract:
Traffic lights, traffic signals, or stoplights – also known as robots in South Africa, Zambia, and Namibia – are signalling devices positioned at road intersections, pedestrian crossings, and other locations to control the flow of traffic.
Traffic lights usually consist of three signals, conveying meaningful information to road users through colours and symb

objects.title: Sausage
objects.extract: A sausage is a type of meat product usually made from ground meat—often pork, beef, or poultry—along with salt, spices and other flavourings. Other ingredients, such as grains or breadcrumbs, may be included as fillers or extenders.
objects.artist: No machine-readable author provided. Ardo Beltz assumed (based on copyright clai

objects.title: Art in bronze and brass
objects.extract: Art in bronze and brass dates from remote antiquity. These important metals are alloys, bronze composed of copper and tin and brass of copper and zinc.

objects.title: white-nosed coati
objects.extract: The white-nosed coati (Nasua narica), also known as the coatimundi (), is a species of coati and a member of the family Procyonidae (raccoons and their relatives). Local Spanish names for the species include antoon, gato solo, pizote, and tejón, depending upon the region.

objects.title: Baklava
objects.extract:
Baklava ( , or ) is a layered pastry dessert made of filo pastry, filled with chopped nuts, and sweetened with either syrup or honey.
There are several theories for the origin of this pastry, but in modern times, it is a common dessert among cuisines of countries in West Asia, Southeast Europe, Central Asia, and North Africa.

objects.title: Mammal
objects.description: Class of animals with milk-producing glands
objects.extract: A mammal (from Latin  mamma 'breast') is a vertebrate animal of the class Mammalia (). Mammals are characterized by the presence of milk-producing mammary glands for feeding their young, a broad neocortex region of the brain, fur or hair, and three middle ear bones.

objects.title: Lacquerware
objects.extract: Lacquerware are objects decoratively covered with lacquer. Lacquerware includes small or large containers, tableware, miscellaneous lacquered small objects carried by people, and larger objects such as furniture and even coffins painted with lacquer.

objects.title: Casio
objects.extract: Casio Computer Co., Ltd. (カシオ計算機株式会社, Kashio Keisanki Kabushiki-gaisha) is a Japanese multinational electronics manufacturing corporation headquartered in Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan.

objects.title: Truck
objects.extract: A truck (North American and Australian English) or lorry (British English) is a motor vehicle designed to transport freight, carry specialized payloads, or perform other utilitarian work. Trucks vary greatly in size, power, and configuration, but the vast majority feature body-on-frame construction, with a cabin that is independent of the payload portion of

objects.title: Ferris wheel
objects.extract: A Ferris wheel (also called a big wheel, giant wheel or an observation wheel) is an amusement ride consisting of a rotating upright wheel with multiple passenger-carrying components (commonly referred to as passenger cars, cabins, tubs, gondolas, capsules, or pods) attached to the rim in such a way that as the wheel turns, they are kept upright, usually by g
objects.artist: Rodrigo Paredes from Ciudad Autónoma de Buenos Aires, Argentina

objects.title: Ritual Wine Container (Jia)
objects.description: wine container at the Metropolitan Museum of Art (MET, 24.72.7)

objects.title: Cranberry
objects.description: Group of plant species bearing edible fruit
objects.extract: Cranberries are a group of evergreen dwarf shrubs or trailing vines in the subgenus Oxycoccus of the genus Vaccinium. Cranberries are low, creeping shrubs or vines up to 2 meters (7 ft) long and 5 to 20 centimeters (2 to 8 in) in height; they have slender stems that are not thickly woody and have small evergreen leaves.

objects.title: Sabatinca aenea
objects.description: Species of moth endemic to New Zealand
objects.extract: Sabatinca aenea, also known as the Banks Peninsula Metallic, is a species of moth belonging to the family Micropterigidae.  This species was first described by George Hudson in 1923.

objects.title: Zabaione
objects.extract: Zabaione (Italian: [dzabaˈjoːne]) or, through hypercorrection, zabaglione (UK: , US: ; Italian: [dzabaʎˈʎoːne]), is an Italian dessert, or sometimes a drink, made with egg yolks, sugar, and a sweet wine (usually Moscato d'Asti). Some versions of the recipe incorporate spirits such as cognac.

objects.title: Rubber chicken
objects.extract: A rubber chicken is a prop used in comedy. The phrase is also used as a description for food served at speeches, conventions, and other large meetings, and as a metaphor for speechmaking.

objects.title: Gravity
objects.extract:
In physics, gravity (from Latin  gravitas 'weight'), also known as gravitation or a gravitational interaction, is a fundamental interaction, which may be described as the force that draws material objects towards each other.
The gravitational attraction between clouds of primordial hydrogen and clumps of dark matter in the early universe caused the hydrogen

objects.title: Chocolate coin
objects.description: Gold foil covered chocolates in the shape of coins
objects.extract: Chocolate coins or chocolate money are foil-covered chocolates in the shape of coins. They are usually created with milk chocolate and are traditionally packaged in small mesh bags.

objects.title: Basking shark
objects.extract: The basking shark (Cetorhinus maximus) is the second-largest living shark and fish, after the whale shark. It is one of three plankton-eating shark species, along with the whale shark and megamouth shark.

objects.title: Semi-trailer truck
objects.description: Combination of a tractor unit and one or more semi-trailers to carry freight
objects.extract: A semi-trailer truck (also known by a wide variety of other terms – see below) is the combination of a tractor unit and one or more semi-trailers to carry freight. A semi-trailer attaches to the tractor with a type of hitch called a fifth wheel.

objects.title: New York Fashion Week
objects.extract: New York Fashion Week (NYFW), held in February and September of each year, is a semi-annual series of events in Manhattan typically spanning seven to nine days when international fashion collections are shown to buyers, the press, and the general public. It is one of four major fashion weeks in the world, collectively known as the "Big Four", along with thos

objects.title: Atari
objects.extract: Atari () is a brand name that has been owned by several entities since its inception in 1972. It is currently owned by French holding company Atari SA (formerly Infogrames) and its focus is on "video games, consumer hardware, and licensing".

objects.title: Wasp
objects.extract: A wasp is any insect of the narrow-waisted suborder Apocrita of the order Hymenoptera which is neither a bee nor an ant; this excludes the broad-waisted sawflies (Symphyta), which look somewhat like wasps, but are in a separate suborder. The wasps do not constitute a clade, a complete natural group with a single ancestor, as  bees and ants are deeply nested

objects.title: Étagère
objects.description: furniture highlighted in The MET collection

objects.title: Baetis golanensis
objects.artist: Yanai Z, Gattolliat J-L, Dorchin N (2018) Taxonomy of Baetis Leach in Israel (Ep

objects.title: Bronze Age
objects.extract: The Bronze Age is an archaeological and anthropological term defining a phase in the development of material culture among ancient societies in Asia, the Near East and Europe. An ancient civilisation or culture is deemed to be part of the Bronze Age if it either produced bronze by smelting its own copper and alloying it with tin, arsenic, or other metals, or

objects.title: Buntal hat
objects.description: Traditional straw hat from the Philippines
objects.extract: The buntal hat is a traditional lightweight straw hat from the Philippines made from very finely-woven fibers extracted from the petioles of buri palm leaves. It is traditionally worn by farmers working in the fields and was a major export of the Philippines in the first half of the 20th century.

objects.title: Headlamp
objects.description: Lamp mounted in the front of a vehicle
objects.extract: A headlamp is a lamp attached to the front of a vehicle to illuminate the road ahead. Headlamps are also often called headlights, but in United States usage, headlamp is the term for the device itself and headlight is the term for the beam of light produced and distributed by the device.

objects.title: Monster truck
objects.extract: A monster truck is a specialized off-road vehicle with a heavy duty suspension, four-wheel steering, large-displacement V8 engines, and oversized tires constructed for motorsports and entertainment uses. Originally created by modifying stock pickup trucks and sport utility vehicles (SUVs), they have evolved into purpose-built vehicles with tube-frame chassis

objects.title: Honda Super Cub
objects.extract:
The Honda Super Cub (or Honda Cub) is an underbone motorcycle manufactured and marketed by Honda, featuring a four-stroke single-cylinder engine ranging in displacement from 49 to 124 cc (3.0 to 7.6 cu in).
In continuous manufacture since 1958 with production surpassing 60 million in 2008, 87 million in 2014, and 100 million in 2017, the Super Cub is the mos

objects.title: Fig
objects.description: Species of flowering plant, or its fruit
objects.extract: The fig is the edible fruit of Ficus carica (the common fig), a species of tree or shrub in the flowering plant family Moraceae, native to the Mediterranean region, and to western and southern Asia. It has been cultivated since ancient times and is now widely grown throughout the world.

objects.title: Puff pastry
objects.extract: Puff pastry, also known as pâte feuilletée, is a light, flaky pastry, its base dough (détrempe) composed of wheat flour and water. Butter or other solid fat (beurrage) is then layered into the dough.

objects.title: Modesty
objects.description: Dress or behavior to avoid sexual attraction
objects.extract: Modesty, sometimes known as demureness, is a mode of dress and deportment which intends to avoid the encouraging of sexual attraction in others. The word modesty comes from the Latin word modestus which means 'keeping within measure'.

objects.title: Cochineal
objects.description: Species of insect producing the crimson dye carmine
objects.extract: The cochineal ( KOTCH-in-EEL, -⁠eel, US also  KOH-chin-EEL, -⁠eel; Dactylopius coccus) is a scale insect in the suborder Sternorrhyncha, from which the natural dye carmine is derived. A primarily sessile parasite native to tropical and subtropical South America through North America (Mexico and the Southwest United States), this insect lives on cacti in the

objects.title: Microphone
objects.description: Device that converts sound into an electrical signal
objects.extract: A microphone, colloquially called a mic (), or mike, is a transducer that converts sound into an electrical signal. Microphones are used in telecommunication, sound recording, broadcasting, and consumer electronics, including telephones, hearing aids, and mobile devices.

objects.title: Lowell, Massachusetts
objects.extract: Lowell ( ) is a city in Massachusetts, United States. Alongside Cambridge, it is one of the two traditional seats of Middlesex County.

objects.title: Skirt
objects.description: Clothing worn from the waist or hips
objects.extract:
A skirt is the lower part of a dress or a separate outer garment that covers a person from the waist downwards.
At its simplest, a skirt can be a draped garment made out of a single piece of fabric (such as pareos).

objects.title: Eggplant
objects.extract: Eggplant (in North American, Australian, and Philippine English), aubergine (in British, Irish, and New Zealand English), brinjal (in Indian, Bangladeshi, Pakistani, Singapore, Malaysian, South African, and Sri Lankan English), or baigan (in Caribbean English) is a plant species in the nightshade family Solanaceae. Solanum melongena is grown worldwide for it

objects.title: Horchata
objects.extract: Horchata (; Spanish: [oɾˈtʃata] ), or orxata (Valencian: [oɾˈtʃata]), is a name given to various beverages that are generally plant-based but sometimes contain milk. In Spain, it is made with soaked, ground, and sweetened tiger nuts.

objects.title: Icelandic Sheepdog
objects.extract: The Icelandic Sheepdog (Icelandic: Íslenskur fjárhundur, pronounced [ˈistlɛnskʏr ˈfjaurˌhʏntʏr̥]), is an Icelandic breed of dog of Nordic Spitz type. It derives from dogs brought to Iceland by Viking settlers in the ninth century; it is both similar and closely related to the Buhund of Norway and the Vallhund and Norrbottenpets of Sweden, which derive from t

objects.title: Road train
objects.extract: A road train, also known as a land train or long combination vehicle (LCV), is a semi-trailer truck used to move road freight more efficiently than single-trailer semi-trailers. It consists of two semi-trailers or more connected together with or without a prime mover.

objects.title: Thermometer
objects.extract: A thermometer is a device that measures temperature (the hotness or coldness of an object) or temperature gradient (the rates of change of temperature in space). A thermometer has two important elements: (1) a temperature sensor (e.g.

objects.title: chashushuli
objects.description: Georgian dish with meat and tomatoes
objects.extract: Chashushuli (Georgian: ჩაშუშული - "stew") is a dish of Georgian cuisine. The meat (veal in the original recipe) is fried and then stewed with tomatoes.

objects.title: Korean cuisine
objects.extract: Korean cuisine is the set of foods and culinary styles which are associated with Korean culture. This cuisine has evolved through centuries of social and political change.

objects.title: Aenetus ligniveren
objects.extract: Aenetus ligniveren, the common splendid ghost moth,  is a moth in the family Hepialidae. It is found from southern Queensland to Tasmania.

objects.title: Raspberry
objects.extract: The raspberry is the edible fruit of several plant species in the genus Rubus of the rose family, most of which are in the subgenus Idaeobatus. The name also applies to these plants themselves.

objects.title: Brinquedo - Carro Chevrolet Sedan Luxo com Caixa Original
objects.description: item of the collection of toys of the Museu Paulista

objects.title: Fistball
objects.extract: Fistball is a sport of European origin, primarily played in the German-speaking nations of Austria, Germany and Switzerland, as well as in Brazil. The objective of the game is similar to volleyball, in that teams try to hit a ball over a net, but the rules vary from volleyball in several major ways.

objects.title: Stilts
objects.description: Poles used for circus performance, pastime, or work
objects.extract:
Stilts are poles, rods, posts or pillars that allow a person or structure to stand at a height above the ground.
Stilts for walking have platforms for the feet and may be strapped to the user's legs.

objects.title: Cessna 172
objects.extract: The Cessna 172 Skyhawk is an American four-seat, single-engine, high wing, fixed-wing aircraft made by the Cessna Aircraft Company. First flown in 1955, more 172s have been built than any other aircraft.

objects.title: Pakhavaj
objects.extract: The pakhavaj is a barrel-shaped, two-headed drum, originating from the Indian subcontinent,  kendang of Maritime Southeast Asia and other South Asian double-headed drums. Its older forms were made with clay.

objects.title: Excavator
objects.extract:
Excavators are heavy construction equipment primarily consisting of a boom, dipper (or stick), bucket, and cab on a rotating platform known as the "house".
The modern excavator's house sits atop an undercarriage with tracks or wheels, being an evolution of the steam shovel (which itself evolved into the power shovel when steam was replaced by diesel and elec

objects.title: Steering
objects.description: Control of the direction of motion of vehicles and other objects
objects.extract: Steering is the control of the direction of motion or the components that enable its control. Steering is achieved through various arrangements, among them ailerons for airplanes, rudders for boats, cylic tilting of rotors for helicopters, and many more.

objects.title: Helios
objects.description: Greek god and personification of the Sun
objects.extract: In ancient Greek religion and mythology, Helios (; Ancient Greek: Ἥλιος pronounced [hɛ̌ːlios], lit. 'Sun'; Homeric Greek: Ἠέλιος) is the god who personifies the Sun. His name is also Latinized as Helius, and he is often given the epithets Hyperion ("the one above") and Phaethon ("the shining").

objects.title: Giant van Gogh Painting
objects.description: sculpture of painting on easel in Altona, Manitoba, Canada

objects.title: Goryeo
objects.extract: Goryeo (Korean: 고려; Hanja: 高麗; MR: Koryŏ; pronounced [ko.ɾjʌ]; Middle Korean: 고ᇢ롕〮, romanized: kwòwlyéy) was a Korean state founded in 918, during a time of national division called the Later Three Kingdoms period, that unified and ruled the Korean Peninsula until the establishment of Joseon in 1392. Goryeo achieved what has been called a "true national unif

objects.title: Flea
objects.extract: Flea, the common name for the order Siphonaptera, includes 2,500 species of small flightless insects that live as external parasites of mammals and birds. Fleas live by ingesting the blood of their hosts.
objects.artist: Olha Schedrina / The Natural History Museum

objects.title: Ancient Egypt
objects.description: Cradle of civilization in North Africa
objects.extract: Ancient Egypt was a cradle of civilization concentrated along the lower reaches of the Nile River in the eastern corner of North Africa. It emerged from prehistoric Egypt around 3150 BC (according to conventional Egyptian chronology), when Upper and Lower Egypt were united by Menes, who is believed by the majority of Egyptologists to have been the same perso

objects.title: UEFA Conference League
objects.extract: The UEFA Conference League (UECL), usually known simply as the Conference League, is an annual  association football competition organised since 2021 by the Union of European Football Associations (UEFA) for eligible European football clubs. It is the third-tier competition of European club football, ranking below the second-tier UEFA Europa League, and the

objects.title: Sedan (automobile)
objects.description: Passenger car in a three-box configuration
objects.extract: A sedan (American English), or saloon (British English), is a passenger car in a three-box configuration with separate compartments for an engine, passengers, and cargo. Variations of the sedan style include the close-coupled sedan, club sedan, convertible sedan, fastback sedan, hardtop sedan, notchback sedan, and sedanet.

objects.title: Sapato de Bebê de Boneca (1 Pé)
objects.description: Item of the collection of toys of the Museu Paulista - 2 of 5

objects.title: Cessna 172
objects.extract: The Cessna 172 Skyhawk is an American four-seat, single-engine, high wing, fixed-wing aircraft made by the Cessna Aircraft Company. First flown in 1955, more 172s have been built than any other aircraft.

objects.title: House painter and decorator
objects.description: Tradesman who paints and decorates buildings
objects.extract: A house painter and decorator is a tradesperson responsible for the painting and decorating of buildings, and is also known as a decorator, or house painter. The purpose of painting is to improve the appearance of a building and to protect it from damage by water, corrosion, insects and mold.

objects.title: Goldfish
objects.extract: The goldfish (Carassius auratus) is a freshwater fish in the family Cyprinidae of the order Cypriniformes. It is commonly kept as a pet in indoor aquariums, and is one of the most popular aquarium fish.
objects.artist:
パンダチョウビ.jpg: C Lion hiro
derivative work: LK (talk)

objects.title: Cockpit
objects.description: Room from which a pilot controls an aircraft or spacecraft
objects.extract:
A cockpit, also called flight deck, is the area, on the front part of an aircraft, spacecraft, or submersible, from which a pilot controls the vehicle.

The cockpit of an aircraft contains flight instruments on an instrument panel, and the controls that enable the pilot to fly the aircraft.

objects.title: Relief
objects.extract: Relief is a sculptural method in which the sculpted pieces remain attached to a solid background of the same material. The term relief is from the Latin verb relevare, to raise (lit. 'to lift back').

objects.title: Jump seat
objects.extract: A jump seat (sometimes spelled jumpseat) is an auxiliary seat in an automobile, train or aircraft, typically folding or spring-loaded to collapse out of the way when not used.  The term originated in the United States c. 1860 for a movable carriage seat.

objects.title: Herb
objects.description: Plant used for food, medicine or perfume
objects.extract: Herbs are a widely distributed and widespread group of plants, excluding vegetables, with savory or aromatic properties that are used for flavoring and garnishing food, for medicinal purposes, or for fragrances. Culinary use typically distinguishes herbs from spices.

objects.title: Tube (container)
objects.extract: A tube, squeeze tube, or collapsible tube is a collapsible package which can be used for viscous liquids such as toothpaste, artist's paint, adhesive, caulk,  & ointments. Basically, a tube is a cylindrical, hollow piece with a round or oval profile, made of plastic, paperboard, aluminium, or other metal.

objects.title: List of agricultural machinery
objects.extract: Agricultural equipment is any kind of machinery used on a farm to help with farming. The best-known example of this kind is the tractor.

objects.title: Lichen
objects.extract: A lichen ( LY-kən, UK also  LITCH-ən) is a hybrid colony of algae or cyanobacteria living symbiotically among filaments of multiple fungus species, along with bacteria embedded in the cortex, or "skin", in a mutualistic relationship. Lichens are the lifeform that first brought the term symbiosis (as Symbiotismus) into biological context.

objects.title: Wine bottle
objects.extract: A wine bottle is a bottle, generally a glass bottle, that is used for holding wine. Some wines are fermented in the bottle while others are bottled only after fermentation.

objects.title: Cream pie
objects.extract:
A cream pie, crème pie, or creme pie is a type of pie filled with a rich custard or pudding that is made from milk, cream, sugar, wheat flour, and eggs and typically topped with whipped cream.
Cream pies are usually what is used for pieing, or throwing a pie in someone's face.
objects.artist: Seth Lemmons from Boise, Unites States

objects.title: Black-throated Brilliant
objects.extract: The black-throated brilliant (Heliodoxa schreibersii) is a species of hummingbird in tribe Heliantheini of subfamily Lesbiinae. It lives in Brazil, Colombia, Ecuador and Peru.

objects.title: Iguana
objects.extract: Iguana (, Spanish: [iˈɣwana]) is a genus of herbivorous lizards that are native to tropical areas of Mexico, Central America, South America, and the Caribbean. The genus was first described by Austrian naturalist J.N. Laurenti in 1768.

objects.title: Throne
objects.description: Seat of state of a potentate or dignitary
objects.extract: A throne is the seat of state of a potentate or dignitary, especially the seat occupied by a sovereign (or viceroy) on state occasions; or the seat occupied by a pope or bishop on ceremonial occasions. "Throne" in an abstract sense can also refer to the monarchy itself, an instance of metonymy, and is also used in many expressions such as "the power behind t

objects.title: Chariot
objects.description: Carriage using animals to provide rapid motive power
objects.extract: A chariot is a type of vehicle similar to a cart, driven by a charioteer, usually using horses to provide rapid motive power. The oldest known chariots have been found in burials of the Sintashta culture in modern-day Chelyabinsk Oblast, Russia, dated to c.

objects.title: Apple pie
objects.extract: An apple pie is a pie in which the principal filling is apples. It is generally double-crusted, with pastry both above and below the filling; the upper crust may be solid or latticed (woven of crosswise strips).

objects.title: List of British cheeses
objects.extract: This is a list of cheeses from the United Kingdom.

objects.title: Glass square bottle
objects.description: bottle, square at the Metropolitan Museum of Art (MET, 81.10.31)

objects.title: Slate-coloured Hawk
objects.extract: The slate-colored hawk (Buteogallus schistaceus) is a species of bird of prey in the family Accipitridae: the hawks, eagles, and allies. It is found in the Amazon rainforest in Brazil, Bolivia, Peru, Ecuador, Colombia, Venezuela, and French Guiana.

objects.title: Trillium ovatum
objects.extract: Trillium ovatum, commonly known as the Pacific trillium, western wakerobin, western white trillium, or western trillium, is a species of flowering plant in the family Melanthiaceae. It is the most widespread and abundant trillium in western North America.

objects.title: Bombus mixtus
objects.extract: Bombus mixtus is a species of bumblebee. It is native to western North America, where it occurs in western Canada and the United States.

objects.title: Herbal tea
objects.description: Beverage made from infusing or decocting plant material in hot water
objects.extract: Herbal teas or herb teas, technically known as herbal infusions, and less commonly called tisanes, are beverages made from the infusion or decoction in water of any herbs, spices, or other plant material that is not derived from the tea plant (Camellia sinensis). Many herbs used in teas/tisanes are also used in herbal medicine and in folk medicine.
objects.artist: A Girl With Tea from USA

objects.title: Advent calendar
objects.description: Special calendar used to count the days of Advent in anticipation of Christmas
objects.extract: An Advent calendar, from the German word Adventskalender, is used to count the days of Advent in anticipation of Christmas. Since the date of the First Sunday of Advent varies, falling between November 27 and December 3 inclusive, many reusable Advent calendars made of paper or wood begin on December 1.

objects.title: Diadema mexicanum
objects.extract: Diadema mexicanum is a species of long-spined sea urchin belonging to the family Diadematidae. It is native to the Pacific coast of Mexico, Costa Rica, El Salvador, Nicaragua and Panama.

objects.title: CAF Confederation Cup
objects.description: annual football competition organized by the Confederation of African Football

objects.title: Parfait
objects.extract: Parfait ( par-FAY, UK also  PAR-fay, French: [paʁfɛ] ; meaning "perfect") is either of two types of dessert. In France, where the dish originated, parfait is made by boiling cream, egg, sugar and syrup to create a custard-like or meringue-like puree which is then frozen.

objects.title: Barometer
objects.description: Scientific instrument used to measure atmospheric pressure
objects.extract: A barometer is a scientific instrument that is used to measure air pressure. Pressure tendency, which is derived from barometric readings, can forecast short term changes in the weather.

objects.title: Garden furniture
objects.description: Furniture specifically designed for outdoor use
objects.extract: Garden furniture, also called patio furniture or outdoor furniture, is a type of furniture specifically designed for outdoor use.  It is typically made of weather-resistant materials such as aluminium, which is rust-proof.

objects.title: Bump Hadley
objects.extract: Irving Darius Hadley (July 5, 1904 – February 15, 1963) was an American professional baseball pitcher who played in Major League Baseball. Born in Lynn, Massachusetts, he played in the major leagues for the Washington Senators (1926–31 and 1935), Chicago White Sox (1932), St.

objects.title: Pokémon
objects.extract: Pokémon is a Japanese media franchise consisting of video games, animated series and films, a  trading card game, and other related media. The franchise takes place in a shared universe in which humans co-exist with the eponymous creatures, a large variety of species which have special powers.

objects.title: Impeller
objects.description: Rotor that increases fluid pressure and flow
objects.extract: An impeller, or impellor, is a driven rotor used to increase the pressure and flow of a fluid. It is the opposite of a turbine, which extracts energy from, and reduces the pressure of, a flowing fluid.

objects.title: Polkagris
objects.extract: Polkagris (plural: polkagrisar) is a Swedish stick candy that was invented in 1859 by Amalia Eriksson in the town of Gränna, Sweden. It remains a well-known albeit old-fashioned candy in Sweden, often sold at fairs, Christmas markets, and the like.

objects.title: Containerization
objects.extract: Containerization is a system of intermodal freight transport using intermodal containers (also called shipping containers, or ISO containers). Containerization, also referred as container stuffing or container loading, is the process of unitization of cargoes in exports.

objects.title: Bat-and-ball games
objects.description: Field games played by two opposing teams
objects.extract: Bat-and-ball games, or safe haven games, are field games played by two opposing teams. Typically, action starts when the fielding team (at the defense) delivers a ball toward a dedicated player of the batting team (at the attack), who tries to hit it with a bat and then run between various safe areas in the field to score runs (points).

objects.title: Tyrolean hat
objects.description: Type of hat from the Alps
objects.extract: The Tyrolean hat (German: Tirolerhut, Italian: cappello alpino), also Tyrolese hat, Bavarian hat or Alpine hat, is a type of headwear that originally came from the Tyrol in the Alps, in what is now part of Austria, Germany, Italy and Switzerland. It is an essential and distinctive element of the local folk costume, or tracht.

objects.title: Lander (spacecraft)
objects.extract: A lander is a spacecraft that descends towards, then comes to rest on the surface of an astronomical body other than Earth. In contrast to an impact probe, which makes a hard landing that damages or destroys the probe upon reaching the surface, a lander makes a soft landing after which the probe remains functional.

objects.title: Railway crane at Burg  train station
Close helmet
objects.description: Helmet that fully encloses the head, with pivoted bevor
objects.extract: The close helmet or close helm is a type of combat helmet that was worn by knights and other men-at-arms in the Late Medieval and Renaissance eras. It was also used by some heavily armoured, pistol-armed cuirassiers into the mid-17th century.

objects.title: Drink can
objects.description: Container specifically made for liquid such as beverages and usually made of aluminum
objects.extract: A drink can (or beverage can) is a metal container with a polymer interior designed to hold a fixed portion of liquid such as carbonated soft drinks, alcoholic drinks, fruit juices, teas, herbal teas, energy drinks, etc. Drink can exteriors are made of aluminum (75% of worldwide production) or tin-plated steel (25% worldwide production) and the interiors coa

objects.title: Baritone horn
objects.extract: The baritone horn, or often simply the baritone, is a valved brass instrument pitched in B♭ in the saxhorn family, employed chiefly in brass, military and concert bands. In North America for most of the 20th century, the term baritone also referred to similar instruments with a wider-bore closer to the euphonium.
objects.artist: University of Edinburgh; photograph by Antonia Reeve

objects.title: Transmission (mechanical device)
objects.extract:
A transmission (also called a gearbox) is a mechanical device invented by Louis Renault (who founded Renault) which uses a gear set—two or more gears working together—to change the speed, direction of rotation, or torque multiplication or reduction, in a machine. 
A transmission can have a single, or fixed, gear ratio or it can have variable ratios; a variab
objects.artist: No machine-readable author provided. Jaypee assumed (based on copyright claims).

objects.title: Bread loaf
objects.description: bread of the Eighteenth Dynasty of Egypt (Egyptian New Kingdom), 1425–1353 BCE, in the collection of Museo Egizio, Turin, Italy (S.8304)

objects.title: Photomontage
objects.description: Composite image created from two or more photographs
objects.extract: Photomontage is the process and the result of making a composite photograph by cutting, gluing, rearranging and overlapping two or more photographs into a new image. Sometimes the resulting composite image is photographed so that the final image may appear as a seamless physical print.

objects.title: Ancient Greece
objects.description: Greek civilization from 1200 BC to 600 AD
objects.extract: Ancient Greece (Ancient Greek: Ἑλλάς, romanized: Hellás) was a northeastern Mediterranean civilization, existing from the Greek Dark Ages of the 12th–9th centuries BC to the end of classical antiquity (c. 600 AD), comprising a loose collection of culturally and linguistically related city-states and communities. Prior to the Roman period, most of these regio

objects.title: Tractor
objects.description: Engineering vehicle specifically designed to deliver a high tractive effort
objects.extract: A tractor is an engineering vehicle specifically designed to deliver a high tractive effort (or torque) at slow speeds, for the purposes of hauling a trailer or machinery, such as that used in agriculture, mining or construction. Most commonly, the term is used to describe a farm vehicle that provides the power and traction to mechanize agricultural tasks, e

objects.title: Capon
objects.extract: A capon (from Latin: cāpō, genitive cāpōnis) is a male chicken that has been castrated or neutered, either physically or chemically, to improve the quality of its flesh for food, and, in some countries like Spain, fattened by forced feeding.

objects.title: Starch
objects.description: Glucose polymer used as energy store in plants
objects.extract: Starch or amylum is a polymeric carbohydrate consisting of numerous glucose units joined by glycosidic bonds. This polysaccharide is produced by most green plants for energy storage.

objects.title: Oolong
objects.extract: Oolong (UK: , US: ; simplified Chinese: 乌龙茶; traditional Chinese: 烏龍茶; pinyin: wūlóngchá; Jyutping: wu1lung4 caa4; Pe̍h-ōe-jī: o͘-liông tê, 'black dragon' tea) is a traditional semi-oxidized Chinese tea (Camellia sinensis) produced through a process that includes withering the leaves under strong sun and allowing some oxidation to occur before curling and tw

objects.title: Fragment of a mummy covering
objects.description: fragmentary cartonnage mummy covering of the Egyptian Late Period – Early Hellenistic Period, 700–300 BCE, in the collection of Museo Egizio, Turin, Italy (Provv.3574)

objects.title: Chia Pet
objects.description: Terracotta figurine used to sprout chia
objects.extract: Chia Pets are American styled terracotta figurines used to sprout chia, where the chia sprouts grow within a couple of weeks to resemble the animal's fur or hair. Moistened chia seeds (Salvia hispanica) are applied to a grooved terracotta figurine.

objects.title: Zetor
objects.extract: Zetor (since January 1, 2007, officially Zetor Tractors a.s.) is a Czech agricultural machinery manufacturer. It was founded in 1946.

objects.title: Le Boréal
objects.extract:
Le Boreal is a cruise ship owned and operated by the French cruise line company Compagnie du Ponant.
She cruises to Antarctica and other places.

objects.title: Semi-trailer truck
objects.description: Combination of a tractor unit and one or more semi-trailers to carry freight
objects.extract: A semi-trailer truck (also known by a wide variety of other terms – see below) is the combination of a tractor unit and one or more semi-trailers to carry freight. A semi-trailer attaches to the tractor with a type of hitch called a fifth wheel.

objects.title: Ureibe bei Steibis
objects.description: Taxus baccata at the foot of the mountain Seelekopf with an estimated age of at not less than 600 years

objects.title: Joan Crawford
objects.extract: Joan Crawford (born Lucille Fay LeSueur; March 23, 1904–1908 – May 10, 1977) was an American actress. She began her career as a dancer in traveling theatrical companies before debuting on Broadway.

objects.title: EDVAC
objects.extract: EDVAC (Electronic Discrete Variable Automatic Computer) was one of the earliest electronic computers. It was built by Moore School of Electrical Engineering at the University of Pennsylvania in the United States.

objects.title: Keyed trumpet
objects.description: Early Classical era trumpet with keys
objects.extract: The keyed trumpet is a cylindrical-bore brass instrument in the trumpet family that makes use of tone holes operated by keys to alter pitch and provide a full chromatic scale, rather than extending the length of tubing with a slide or valves. It was developed from the natural trumpet in the 18th century and reached its high-point in popularity c. 1800 when t

objects.title: Bitis xeropaga
objects.extract: Bitis xeropaga, commonly known as the desert mountain adder or Lüderitz dwarf viper, is a viper species found in southern Namibia and northwestern Cape Province in South Africa. Like all other vipers, it is venomous.

objects.title: Otamatone
objects.extract: The Otamatone (Japanese: オタマトーン, Hepburn: Otamatōn) is an electronic musical synthesizer. Shaped like an eighth note, the ribbon controller on its stem is used to control pitch, while the notes produced by the synthesizer are played from the Otamatone's "head".

objects.title: Prince Frederick, Duke of York and Albany
objects.extract: Prince Frederick, Duke of York and Albany (Frederick Augustus; 16 August 1763 – 5 January 1827), was the second son of George III, King of the United Kingdom and Hanover, and his consort Charlotte of Mecklenburg-Strelitz. A soldier by profession, from 1764 to 1803 he was Prince-Bishop of Osnabrück in the Holy Roman Empire.

objects.title: Brittany
objects.extract: Brittany ( BRIT-ən-ee) is a peninsula, historical region and cultural area in the north-west of modern France, covering the western part of what was known as Armorica in Roman Gaul. It became an independent kingdom and then a duchy before being united with the Kingdom of France in 1532 as a province governed as a separate nation under the crown.

objects.title: Army men
objects.extract: Army men, or plastic soldiers, are toy soldiers that are about 5 cm (2.0 in) tall and most commonly molded from low-density polyethylene plastic, which makes them durable and flexible. Army men are traditionally solid green or tan, and molded with 20th-century uniforms and weapons.

objects.title: Piece of sorghum bread contained in basket S. 283, from the grave goods of child mummy S. 278
objects.description: bread of the Predynastic Period, late 4th millennium BC, 3300–3000 BCE, in the collection of Museo Egizio, Turin, Italy

objects.title: Cabernet Sauvignon
objects.extract: Cabernet Sauvignon (French: [kabɛʁnɛ soviɲɔ̃]) is one of the world's most widely recognized red wine grape varieties. It is grown in nearly every major wine producing country among a diverse spectrum of climates from Australia and  British Columbia, Canada to Lebanon's Beqaa Valley.

objects.title: Skateboarding
objects.extract: Skateboarding is an action sport that involves riding and performing tricks using a skateboard, as well as a recreational activity, an art form, an entertainment industry job, and a method of transportation. Originating in the United States, skateboarding has been shaped and influenced by many skateboarders throughout the years.

objects.title: Camp bed
objects.extract: A camp bed is a narrow, light-weight bed, often made of sturdy cloth stretched over a folding frame. The term camp bed is common in the United Kingdom, but in North America they are often referred to as cots.

objects.title: Tambourine
objects.extract: The tambourine is a musical instrument in the percussion family consisting of a frame, often of wood or plastic, with pairs of small metal jingles, called "zills". Classically the term tambourine denotes an instrument with a drumhead, though some variants may not have a head.

objects.title: Skewer
objects.description: Thin metal or wood stick used to hold pieces of food together
objects.extract: A skewer is a thin metal or wood stick used to hold pieces of food together. The word may sometimes be used as a metonym, to refer to the entire food item served on a skewer, as in "chicken skewers".

objects.title: Steam engine
objects.description: Engine that uses steam to perform mechanical work
objects.extract: A steam engine is a heat engine that performs mechanical work using steam as its working fluid. The steam engine uses the force produced by steam pressure to push a piston back and forth inside a cylinder.

objects.title: Wild boar
objects.extract: The wild boar (Sus scrofa), also known as the wild swine, common wild pig, Eurasian wild pig, or simply wild pig, is a suid native to much of Eurasia and North Africa, and has been introduced to the Americas and Oceania. The species is now one of the widest-ranging mammals in the world, as well as the most widespread suiform.

objects.title: Light fixture
objects.description: Electrical device with an electric lamp
objects.extract: A light fixture (US English), light fitting (UK English) or luminaire is an electrical lighting device containing one or more light sources, such as light bulbs, and all the accessory components required for its operation to provide illumination to the environment. All light fixtures have a fixture body and one or more light sources.

objects.title: RadioShack
objects.extract: RadioShack (formerly written as Radio Shack) is an American electronics retailer that was established in 1921 as a mail-order business focused on amateur radio. Its parent company was purchased by Tandy Corporation in 1962; Tandy ended mail order, shifted to retail by opening small stores staffed by people who knew electronics, greatly reduced the number of
objects.artist: James Case from Philadelphia, Mississippi, U.S.A.

objects.title: Luxury goods
objects.description: Good for which demand increases more than what is proportional as income rises
objects.extract: In economics, a luxury good (or upmarket good) is a product or item or service  for which demand increases more than what is proportional as income rises, so that expenditures on the good become a more significant proportion of overall spending. Luxury goods are in contrast to necessity goods, where demand increases proportionally less than income.
objects.artist: No machine-readable author provided. CommonsHelper2 Bot assumed (based on copyri

objects.title: Furniture
objects.description: Objects used to support human activities
objects.extract: Furniture refers to objects intended to support various human activities, such as seating (e.g., stools, chairs, and sofas), eating (tables), storing items, working, and sleeping (e.g., beds and hammocks). Furniture is also used to hold objects at a convenient height for work (as horizontal surfaces above the ground, such as tables and desks), or to store th

objects.title: LMS Sentinel 7192
objects.extract: The London, Midland and Scottish Railway (LMS) Sentinel No. 7192 was a geared steam locomotive.

objects.title: Aloe
objects.extract: Aloe (; also written Aloë) is a genus containing over 650 species of flowering succulent plants. The most widely known  species is Aloe vera, or "true aloe".

objects.title: List of culinary fruits
objects.extract: This list contains the names of fruits that are considered edible either raw or cooked in various cuisines. The word fruit is used in several different ways.

objects.title: Coin
objects.description: Small, flat and usually round piece of material used as money
objects.extract: A coin is a small object, usually round and flat, used primarily as a medium of exchange or legal tender. They are standardized in weight, and produced in large quantities at a mint in order to facilitate trade.

objects.title: Durian
objects.extract: The durian ( ) is the edible fruit of several tree species belonging to the genus Durio. There are 30 recognised species, at least nine of which produce edible fruit.

objects.title: French onion soup
objects.extract: French onion soup (French: soupe à l’oignon [sup a lɔɲɔ̃]) is a soup of onions which are sautéed and then cooked in meat stock or water, usually served gratinéed with croutons or a larger piece of bread covered with cheese floating on top. Onion soups have been known in France since medieval times, but the version now familiar dates from the mid-19th century

objects.title: Bombus vosnesenskii
objects.extract: Bombus vosnesenskii, the yellow-faced bumblebee, is a species of bumblebee native to the west coast of North America, where it is distributed from British Columbia to Baja California. It is the most abundant species of bee in this range, and can be found in both urban and agricultural areas.
objects.artist: Kevin Cole from Pacific Coast, USA (en:User:Kevinlcole)

objects.title: Victorinox
objects.extract: Victorinox () is a knife manufacturer and watchmaker based in the town of Ibach, in the Canton of Schwyz, Switzerland. It is known for its Swiss Army knives.

objects.title: Hammerhead shark
objects.extract: The hammerhead sharks are a group of sharks that form the family Sphyrnidae, named for the unusual and distinctive form of their heads, which are flattened and laterally extended into a cephalofoil (a T-shape or "hammer"). The shark's eyes are placed one on each end of this T-shaped structure, with their small mouths directly centered and underneath.

objects.title: Toothpick
objects.extract: A toothpick is a small thin stick of wood, plastic, bamboo, metal, bone or other substance with at least one and sometimes two pointed ends to insert between teeth to remove detritus, usually after a meal. Toothpicks are also used for festive occasions to hold or spear small appetizers (like cheese cubes or olives) or as a cocktail stick, and can be decorate

objects.title: Victorian fashion
objects.description: Fashions and trends in British culture during the Victorian era
objects.extract: Victorian fashion consists of the various fashions and trends in British culture that emerged and developed in the United Kingdom and the British Empire throughout the Victorian era, roughly from the 1830s through the 1890s. The period saw many changes in fashion, including changes in styles, fashion technology and the methods of distribution.

objects.title: Heavy equipment
objects.description: Vehicles designed for executing construction tasks
objects.extract: Heavy equipment, heavy machinery, earthmovers, construction vehicles, or construction equipment, refers to heavy-duty vehicles specially designed to execute construction tasks, most frequently involving earthwork operations or other large construction tasks. Heavy equipment usually comprises five equipment systems: the implement, traction, structure, power t

objects.title: Transistor radio
objects.extract: A transistor radio is a small portable radio receiver that uses transistor-based circuitry.  Previous portable radios used vacuum tubes, which were bulky, fragile, had a limited lifetime, consumed excessive power and required large, heavy batteries.

objects.title: Surfboard
objects.description: Platform board used in the sport of surfing
objects.extract: A surfboard is a narrow plank used in surfing. Surfboards are relatively light, but are strong enough to support an individual standing on them while riding an ocean wave.

objects.title: GI glasses
objects.description: Eyeglasses issued by the American military to its service members
objects.extract: GI glasses are eyeglasses issued by the American military to its service members. Dysphemisms for them include the most common "birth control glasses" (BCGs) and other variants.

objects.title: Pasta
objects.description: Cooked dough food in Italian cuisine
objects.extract: Pasta (UK: , US: ; Italian: [ˈpasta]) is a type of food typically made from an unleavened dough of wheat flour mixed with water or eggs, and formed into sheets or other shapes, then cooked by boiling or baking. Pasta was originally only made with durum, although the definition has been expanded to include alternatives for a gluten-free diet, such as rice flo

objects.title: Fire engine
objects.description: Emergency vehicle intended to put out fires
objects.extract: A fire engine (or fire truck, also spelled firetruck) is a vehicle, usually a specially designed or modified truck, that functions as a firefighting apparatus. The primary purposes of a fire engine include transporting firefighters and water to an incident as well as carrying equipment for firefighting operations in a fire drill.

objects.title: Super FX
objects.description: 3D graphics chip used in Super Nintendo games
objects.extract: The Super FX is a coprocessor on the Graphics Support Unit (GSU) added to select Super Nintendo Entertainment System (SNES) video game cartridges, primarily to facilitate advanced 2D and 3D graphics.

objects.title: Toy piano
objects.extract: The toy piano, also known as the kinderklavier (lit. 'children's piano' in German), is a small piano-like musical instrument. Most modern toy pianos use round metal rods, as opposed to strings in a regular piano, to produce sound.

objects.title: Pot pie
objects.extract: A pot pie or potpie  is a type of savory pie, usually a meat pie, covered by a pie crust consisting of flaky pastry. Pot pies may be made with a variety of fillings including poultry, beef, seafood or plant-based meat substitute fillings, and may also differ in the types of crust.

objects.title: Polo
objects.extract:
Polo is a stick and ball game that is played on horseback as a traditional field sport, and is one of the oldest known team sports in the world. 
Outdoor or "field" polo is played on a 300-by-160-yard grass field, while indoor or "arena" polo is played on a smaller 300-by-150-foot dirt arena.

objects.title: V-1 flying bomb
objects.description: German World War II cruise missile
objects.extract: The V-1 flying bomb (German: Vergeltungswaffe 1, lit. 'Vengeance Weapon 1') was an early cruise missile. Its official Reich Aviation Ministry (RLM) name was Fieseler Fi 103 and its suggestive name was Höllenhund (hellhound).

objects.title: basket winding
objects.extract: Basket winding (or basket-weave winding or honeycomb winding or scatter winding) is a winding method for electrical wire in a coil. The winding pattern is used for radio-frequency electronic components with many parallel wires, such as inductors and transformers.

objects.title: Lowell, Massachusetts
objects.extract: Lowell ( ) is a city in Massachusetts, United States. Alongside Cambridge, it is one of the two traditional seats of Middlesex County.

objects.title: Fendt
objects.extract: Fendt is a German agricultural machinery manufacturer founded in 1930 by Xaver Fendt in Marktoberdorf in the Allgäu region of Bavaria, Germany. Fendt manufactures tractors, combine harvesters, forage harvesters, balers, telescopic handlers and row crop planters.

objects.title: Kong toch
objects.extract: The kong vong toch (Khmer: គងវង់តូច or kong touch Khmer: គងតូច) is a number of gongs that are attached to a circle-shaped rack, closely resembling its larger relative, the kong thom. Both instruments belong to the percussion family of traditional Khmer instruments, along with the roneat ek, roneat dek, and roneat thung.

objects.title: Orange (fruit)
objects.extract: The orange, also called sweet orange to distinguish it from the bitter orange (Citrus × aurantium), is the fruit of a tree in the family Rutaceae. Botanically, this is the hybrid Citrus × sinensis, between the pomelo (Citrus maxima) and the mandarin orange (Citrus reticulata).
objects.artist: Clemson University - USDA Cooperative Extension Slide Series, Bugwood.org

objects.title: Vise
objects.extract: A vise or vice (British English) is a mechanical apparatus used to secure an object to allow work to be performed on it. Vises have two parallel jaws, one fixed and the other movable, threaded in and out by a screw and lever.

objects.title: Aluminium foil
objects.extract: Aluminium foil (or aluminum foil in American English; also called tinfoil) is aluminium prepared in thin metal leaves. The foil is pliable and can be readily bent or wrapped around objects.
objects.artist: Gila Brand, aka Gilabrand at en.wikipedia

objects.title: Maraca
objects.extract: A maraca ( mə-RAK-ə, US also  mə-RAH-kə, Brazilian Portuguese: [maˈɾakɐ] ), sometimes called shaker or chac-chac, is a rattle which appears in many genres of Caribbean and Latin music. It is shaken by a handle and usually played as part of a pair.

objects.title: Endoclita damor
objects.extract: Endoclita damor is a species of moth of the family Hepialidae. It is known from India and the Himalayas.

objects.title: Wearable technology
objects.description: Clothing and accessories incorporating computer and advanced electronic technologies
objects.extract: Wearable technology is a category of small electronic and mobile devices with wireless communications capability designed to be worn on the human body and are incorporated into gadgets, accessories, or clothes. Common types of wearable technology include smartwatches, fitness trackers, and smartglasses.

objects.title: Handcar
objects.description: Railroad car powered by its passengers
objects.extract: A handcar (also known as a pump trolley, pump car, rail push trolley, push-trolley, jigger, Kalamazoo, velocipede, gandy dancer cart, platelayers' cart, draisine, or railbike) is a railroad car powered by its passengers or by people pushing the car from behind. It is mostly used as a railway maintenance of way or mining car, but it was also used for passenge

objects.title: Under Armour
objects.description: American sports clothing and accessories company
objects.extract: Under Armour, Inc. is an American sportswear company that manufactures footwear and apparel headquartered in Baltimore, Maryland, United States.

objects.title: Motorcycle boot
objects.description: Boots worn to protect a motorcyclist
objects.extract: Motorcycle boots are associated with motorcycle riders and range from above ankle to below knee boots.  They have an outside of a typical boot but a low heel to control the motorcycle.

objects.title: Ghatam
objects.extract: The ghaṭam (Sanskrit: घट ghaṭ; Kannada: ಘಟ ghaṭa; Tamil: கடம் ghatam; Telugu: ఘటం ghataṃ; Malayalam: ഘടം ghataṃ) is a percussion instrument used in various repertoires across the Indian subcontinent, especially in Southern India . Its variant is played in Punjab and known as gharha as it is a part of Punjabi folk traditions.

objects.title: Helicopter
objects.extract: A helicopter is a type of rotorcraft in which lift and thrust are supplied by horizontally spinning rotors. This allows the helicopter to take off and land vertically, to hover, and to fly forward, backward and laterally.

objects.title: Angle of repose
objects.description: Steepest angle at which granular materials can be piled before slumping
objects.extract: The angle of repose, or critical angle of repose, of a granular material is the steepest angle of descent or dip  relative to the horizontal plane on which the material can be piled without slumping. At this angle, the material on the slope face is on the verge of sliding.
objects.artist: Object made by Estes Objethos Atelier, photo by Rodrigo Tetsuo Argenton

objects.title: Hanbok
objects.extract: The hanbok (Korean: 한복; Hanja: 韓服; lit. 'Korean dress') is the traditional clothing of the Korean people.

objects.title: Soldering iron
objects.extract: A soldering iron is  a hand tool used in soldering. It supplies heat to melt solder so that it can flow into the joint between two workpieces.

objects.title: Incense
objects.description: Material that releases fragrant smoke when burnt
objects.extract: Incense is an aromatic biotic material that releases fragrant smoke when burnt. The term is used for either the material or the aroma.

objects.title: Water metering
objects.extract: Water metering is the practice of measuring water use. Water meters measure the volume of water used by residential and commercial building units that are supplied with water by a public water supply system.

objects.title: Minivan
objects.description: Type of van designed for private use
objects.extract: A minivan, commonly known as a van, is a car classification for vehicles designed to transport passengers in the rear seating row(s), with reconfigurable seats in two or three rows. The equivalent classification in Europe is MPV (multi-purpose vehicle), people carrier, or M-segment.

objects.title: Micropterix aureoviridella
objects.extract: Micropterix aureoviridella is a moth of the family Micropterigidae found in Austria, Germany, Italy, Poland, Slovakia, Slovenia and Switzerland. It was first described by Gabriel Höfner in 1898.

objects.title: Chinese furniture
objects.extract: The forms of Chinese furniture evolved along three distinct lineages which date back to 1000 BC: frame and panel, yoke and rack (based on post-and-rail seen in architecture) and bamboo construction techniques. Chinese home furniture evolved independently of Western furniture into many similar forms, including chairs, tables, stools, cupboards, cabinets, beds

objects.title: Electric generator
objects.description: Device that converts other energy to electrical energy
objects.extract: In electricity generation, a generator, also called an electric generator, electrical generator, and electromagnetic generator is an electromechanical device that converts mechanical energy to electrical energy for use in an external circuit.  In most generators which are rotating machines, a source of kinetic power rotates the generator's shaft, and the gen

objects.title: Ham
objects.description: Pork from a leg cut that has been preserved by wet or dry curing
objects.extract: Ham is pork from a leg cut that has been preserved by wet or dry curing, with or without smoking. As a processed meat, the term ham includes both whole cuts of meat and ones that have been mechanically formed.

objects.title: Sea lion
objects.extract: Sea lions are pinnipeds characterized by external ear flaps, long foreflippers, the ability to walk on all fours, short and thick hair, and a big chest and belly. Together with the fur seals, they make up the family Otariidae, eared seals.

objects.title: Profiterole
objects.extract: A profiterole (French: [pʁɔfitʁɔl]), chou à la crème (French: [ʃu a la kʁɛm]), (known as a cream puff in the US and Canada), is a filled French choux pastry ball with a typically sweet and moist filling of whipped cream, custard, pastry cream, or ice cream. The puffs may be embellished, left plain or garnished with chocolate sauce, caramel, or a dusting of p

objects.title: Water buffalo
