objects.description: Traditional South Dakotan dish similar to shashlik
objects.extract:
Chislic (or sometimes chislick) is a dish consisting of skewered cubes of red meat, usually mutton or lamb, although game meats such as venison and even beef steak can be used. 
Most commonly associated with the state of South Dakota, chislic was declared the official state "nosh" of South Dakota in March 2018.

objects.title: Electric car
objects.description: Car propelled by an electric motor using energy stored in batteries
objects.extract: An electric car or electric vehicle (EV) is an automobile that uses electrical energy as the primary source of propulsion. The term normally refers to a plug-in electric vehicle, typically a battery electric vehicle (BEV), which only uses energy stored in on-board battery packs, but broadly may also include plug-in hybrid electric vehicle (PHEV), range-exten

objects.title: Napkin
objects.description: Absorbent cloth or paper for spills and wiping
objects.extract: A napkin, serviette or face towelette is a square of cloth or paper tissue used at the table for wiping of the mouth and fingers while eating. It is also sometimes used as a bib by tucking it into a shirt collar.

objects.title: Floodlight
objects.description: High-intensity electric light with a broad beam
objects.extract: A floodlight is a broad-beamed, high-intensity artificial light. It can provide functional area lighting for travel-ways, parking, entrances, work areas, and sporting venues to enable visibility adequate for safe task performance, ornamental lighting for advertising, façades, monuments, or support perimeter security.

objects.title: Sabotage
objects.artist: Unknown authorUnknown author or not provided

objects.title: Glastonbury chair
objects.extract: Glastonbury chair is a nineteenth-century term for an earlier wooden chair, usually of oak, possibly based on a chair made for Richard Whiting, the last Abbot of Glastonbury, England. The Glastonbury chair was known to exist since the Early Middle Ages, but seems to have disappeared from use in part of the Later Middle Ages; it re-emerged in use in Italy by

objects.title: Flatbread
objects.extract: A flatbread is bread typically made with flour, water, and salt, with or without leavening, which are mixed and rolled into flattened dough. They are usually baked quickly at high temperatures and range from below one millimeter to a few centimeters in thickness.

objects.title: Bamboo
objects.description: Subfamily of plants in the grass family
objects.extract: Bamboos are a diverse group of mostly evergreen perennial flowering plants making up the subfamily Bambusoideae of the grass family Poaceae. Giant bamboos are the largest members of the grass family, in the case of Dendrocalamus sinicus having individual stalks (culms) reaching a length of 46 meters (151 ft), up to 36 centimeters (14 in) in diameter and a we

objects.title: Bison
objects.extract: A bison (pl.: bison) is a large bovine in the genus Bison (from Greek, meaning 'wild ox') within the tribe Bovini. Two extant and numerous extinct species are recognised.

objects.title: Blowtorch
objects.description: Fuel-burning tool for applying flame and heat for various applications
objects.extract: A blowtorch, also referred to as a blowlamp, is an ambient air fuel-burning tool used for applying flame and heat to various applications, usually in metalworking, but occasionally for foods like crème brûlée.

objects.title: White tie
objects.extract: White tie, also called full evening dress or a dress suit, is the most formal evening Western dress code. For men, it consists of a black tail coat (alternatively referred to as a dress coat, usually by tailors) worn over a white dress shirt with a starched or piqué bib, white piqué waistcoat and the white bow tie worn around a standing wing collar.

objects.title: Clock
objects.description: clock at the Metropolitan Museum of Art (MET, 1983.572)

objects.title: Cougar
objects.description: Wild cat species native to the Americas
objects.extract: The cougar ( KOO-gər; Puma concolor), also called puma, mountain lion, catamount, and panther, is a large feline native to the Americas. It inhabits North, Central and South America, making it the most widely distributed wild, terrestrial mammal in the Western Hemisphere, and one of the most widespread in the world.

objects.title: Bread loaf bound with papyrus strips
objects.description: bread of the Eighteenth Dynasty of Egypt (Egyptian New Kingdom), 1425–1353 BCE, in the collection of Museo Egizio, Turin, Italy (S.8264)

objects.title: Drinking fountain
objects.description: Fountain designed to provide drinking water
objects.extract: A drinking fountain, also called a water fountain or bubbler, is a fountain designed to provide drinking water. It consists of a basin with either continuously running water or a tap.

objects.title: Charybdis japonica
objects.extract: Charybdis japonica, the Asian paddle crab, is a species of swimming crab in the family Portunidae. Charybdis japonica has a hexagonal, concave carapace around 12 centimetres (4.7 in) wide, the whole animal being pale green to olive green in colour.

objects.title: Jennifer Capriati
objects.extract: Jennifer Maria Capriati  (born March 29, 1976) is an American former professional tennis player. She was ranked as the world No.

objects.title: List of microbreweries
objects.extract: This is a list of notable microbreweries. A microbrewery is a brewery which produces a limited amount of beer.

objects.title: Ball
objects.extract: A ball is a round object (usually spherical, but sometimes ovoid). Balls can have several uses.

objects.title: Backhendl
objects.extract: Backhendl or Wiener Backhendl (English: Viennese fried chicken), has been a specialty of Viennese cuisine since the 18th century. It consists of prepared and spiced portions of chicken, which are breaded and crispy deep fried.
objects.artist: Josef Türk Reit im Winkl Chiemgau

objects.title: Apple Vision Pro
objects.extract: The Apple Vision Pro is a head-worn computer developed by Apple. It was announced on June 5, 2023, at Apple's Worldwide Developers Conference (WWDC) and was released first in the US, then in global territories throughout 2024.

objects.title: Sea slug
objects.extract: Sea slug is a common name for some marine invertebrates with varying levels of resemblance to terrestrial slugs. Most creatures known as sea slugs are gastropods, i.e.

objects.title: Chassis dynamometer
objects.description: Roller assembly used for vehicle testing
objects.extract: A chassis dynamometer, informally referred to as a rolling road or a dyno, is a mechanical device that uses one or more fixed roller assemblies to simulate different road conditions within a controlled environment, and is used for a wide variety of vehicle testing and development purposes.

objects.title: Scalpel
objects.description: Sharp bladed instrument used for surgery
objects.extract: A scalpel or bistoury is a small and extremely sharp bladed instrument used for surgery, anatomical dissection, podiatry and various handicrafts. A lancet is a double-edged scalpel.

objects.title: Oberheim OB12
objects.extract:
The Oberheim OB•12 is a Virtual Analog synthesizer, designed and realised by the Italian musical instrument manufacturer Viscount, in production between 2000 and 2005.
The synth used Oberheim brand name under license from Gibson, which bought Oberheim rights after the original company went out of business in 1987.

objects.title: Azulejo
objects.extract: Azulejo (Portuguese: [ɐzuˈle(j)ʒu, ɐzuˈlɐjʒu], Spanish: [aθuˈlexo]; from the Arabic الزليج, al-zillīj) is a form of Iberian painted tin-glazed ceramic tilework. They are an ornamental art form, but also had a specific function, such as aiding temperature control in homes.

objects.title: Hákarl
objects.description: National dish of Iceland consisting of fermented shark
objects.extract: Hákarl (short for kæstur hákarl [ˈkʰaistʏr ˈhauːˌkʰa(r)tl̥]), referred to as fermented shark in English, is a national dish of Iceland consisting of Greenland shark or other sleeper shark that has been cured with a particular fermentation process and hung to dry for four to five months. It has a strong ammonia-rich smell and fishy taste, making hákarl an acq

objects.title: List of food preparation utensils
objects.extract: A kitchen utensil is a hand-held, typically small tool that is designed for food-related functions. Food preparation utensils are a specific type of kitchen utensil, designed for use in the preparation of food.
objects.artist:
Original: MarkusHagenlocher at German Wikipedia
Modifications: Cornischong at Lu

objects.title: Splat (furniture)
objects.extract: A splat is the vertical central element of a chair back. Typically this element of a chair is of exposed wood design.

objects.title: Rocking chair
objects.extract: A rocking chair or rocker is a type of chair with two curved bands (also known as rockers) attached to the bottom of the legs, connecting the legs on each side to each other.  The rockers contact the floor at only two points, giving the occupant the ability to rock back and forth by shifting their weight or pushing lightly with their feet.

objects.title: Road roller
objects.extract: A road roller (sometimes called a roller-compactor, or just roller) is a compactor-type engineering vehicle used to compact soil, gravel, concrete, or asphalt in the construction of roads and foundations. Similar rollers are used also at landfills or in agriculture.

objects.title: Space hopper
objects.extract: A space hopper (also known as a moon hopper, skippyball, kangaroo ball, bouncer, hippity hop, hoppity hop, sit and bounce, or hop ball) is a rubber ball (similar to an exercise ball) with handles that allow one to sit on it without falling off. The user can attempt to hop around on the toy, using its elastic properties to move forward.

objects.title: Tambourine
objects.extract: The tambourine is a musical instrument in the percussion family consisting of a frame, often of wood or plastic, with pairs of small metal jingles, called "zills". Classically the term tambourine denotes an instrument with a drumhead, though some variants may not have a head.

objects.title: Jellyfish
objects.extract: Jellyfish, also known as sea jellies or simply jellies, are the medusa-phase of certain gelatinous members of the subphylum Medusozoa, which is a major part of the phylum Cnidaria. Jellyfish are mainly free-swimming marine animals, although a few are anchored to the seabed by stalks rather than being motile.

objects.title: USS Samuel Chase
objects.extract:
USS Samuel Chase (APA-26), launched as SS African Meteor, was an Arthur Middleton-class attack transport manned by the United States Coast Guard during World War II. She was named after Founding Father Samuel Chase, a signatory to the Declaration of Independence.
Samuel Chase participated in all five of the major U.S. amphibious invasions in the European The

objects.title: Angle of repose
objects.description: Steepest angle at which granular materials can be piled before slumping
objects.extract: The angle of repose, or critical angle of repose, of a granular material is the steepest angle of descent or dip  relative to the horizontal plane on which the material can be piled without slumping. At this angle, the material on the slope face is on the verge of sliding.
objects.artist: Object made by Estes Objethos Atelier, photo by Rodrigo Tetsuo Argenton

objects.title: Brinquedo - Coelhinho Porta-Ovo de Páscoa
objects.description: Item of the collection of toys of the Museu Paulista - 2 of 2

objects.title: Sailboat
objects.description: Boat propelled partly or entirely by sails
objects.extract: A sailboat or sailing boat is a boat propelled partly or entirely by sails and is smaller than a sailing ship. Distinctions in what constitutes a sailing boat and ship vary by region and maritime culture.

objects.title: Mixing console
objects.extract: A mixing console or mixing desk is an electronic device for mixing audio signals, used in sound recording and reproduction and sound reinforcement systems. Inputs to the console include microphones, signals from electric or electronic instruments, or recorded sounds.

objects.title: Onigiri
objects.extract: Onigiri (おにぎり, お握り or 御握り), also known as omusubi (おむすび or お結び) or nigirimeshi (握り飯), is a Japanese rice ball made from white rice. It is usually formed into triangular or cylindrical shapes, and wrapped in nori (seaweed).

objects.title: Miniature Drum with Four Frogs
objects.description: drum at the Metropolitan Museum of Art (MET, 2000.284.57)

objects.title: Black tie
objects.extract: Black tie is a semi-formal Western dress code for evening events, originating in British and North American conventions for attire in the 19th century. In British English, the dress code is often referred to synecdochically by its principal element for men, the dinner suit or dinner jacket.

objects.title: Covered Box in the Form of a Snail
Knox Automobile Company
objects.extract: The Knox Automobile Company was a manufacturer of automobiles in Springfield, Massachusetts, United States, between 1900 and 1914. Knox also built trucks and farm tractors until 1924.

objects.title: Cornettino
objects.description: Descant instrument of the cornetto family
objects.extract: The cornettino (Italian, plural cornettini; German: Diskant Zink, Quart-Zink) is the small descant instrument of the cornett family of lip-reed wind instruments, a fourth or fifth higher than the larger, more common treble cornett. Cornettini were built in two sizes, usually described as in D or C, although the note sounded with all finger-holes covered is E

objects.title: Heath Baby Bullet
objects.extract: The Heath Baby Bullet was a racing aircraft built during the interwar period.

objects.title: Speak & Spell (toy)
objects.description: Electronic toy made by Texas Instruments
objects.extract: The Speak & Spell line is a series of electronic hand-held child computers by Texas Instruments that consisted of a TMC0280 linear predictive coding speech synthesizer, a keyboard, and a receptor slot to receive one of a collection of ROM game library modules. The first Speak & Spell was introduced at the summer Consumer Electronics Show in June 1978 (1978-0

objects.title: Bunting (decoration)
objects.extract: Bunting refers to decorative flags, wide streamers, or draperies made of fabric, or of plastic, paper or cardboard in imitation of fabric. Bunting is also a collection of flags, and the fabric used to make flags.

objects.title: Horse
objects.extract: The horse (Equus ferus caballus) is a domesticated, one-toed, hoofed mammal. It belongs to the taxonomic family Equidae and is one of two extant subspecies of Equus ferus.

objects.title: Bocaccio rockfish
objects.extract: The bocaccio rockfish (Sebastes paucispinis) is a species of marine ray-finned fish belonging to the subfamily Sebastinae, the rockfishes, part of the family Scorpaenidae, It is found in the northeast Pacific Ocean. It is also known as rock salmon, salmon grouper, rockcod, Pacific red snapper, or simply bocaccio.

objects.title: Electronic article surveillance
objects.extract: Electronic article surveillance (EAS) is a type of system used to prevent shoplifting from retail stores, pilferage of books from libraries, or unwanted removal of properties from office buildings. EAS systems typically consist of two components: EAS antennas and EAS tags or labels.

objects.title: Industrial Revolution
objects.extract: The Industrial Revolution, sometimes called the First Industrial Revolution in contrast to the subsequent Second Industrial Revolution, was a transitional period of the global economy toward more widespread, efficient and stable manufacturing processes, succeeding the Second Agricultural Revolution. Beginning in Great Britain around 1760, the Industrial Revo

objects.title: Steel
objects.extract: Steel is an alloy of iron and carbon that demonstrates improved mechanical properties compared to the pure form of iron. Due to its high elastic modulus, yield strength, fracture strength and low raw material cost, steel is one of the most commonly manufactured materials in the world.
objects.artist: No machine-readable author provided. Kompak~commonswiki assumed (based on copyri

objects.title: Doughnut
objects.description: Sweet food made from deep-fried dough
objects.extract: A doughnut or donut is a type of fried or baked good made from leavened dough. It is popular in many countries and is prepared in various forms as a sweet snack that can be homemade or purchased in bakeries, supermarkets, food stalls, and franchised specialty vendors.

objects.title: Airplane
objects.extract: An airplane (American English), or aeroplane (Commonwealth English), informally plane, is a fixed-wing aircraft that is propelled forward by thrust from a jet engine, propeller, or rocket engine. Airplanes come in a variety of sizes, shapes, and wing configurations.

objects.title: Menards
objects.extract: Menard, Inc., doing business as Menards ( mə-NARDZ), is an American big-box home improvement retail chain headquartered in Eau Claire, Wisconsin. It is the third-largest home improvement retailer in the United States (behind Lowe's and Home Depot), with 341 stores in 15 U.S. states, primarily in the Midwest.

objects.title: Mongols
objects.extract: The Mongols are an East Asian ethnic group native to Mongolia and China (Inner Mongolia and other 11 autonomous territories), as well as the republics of Buryatia and Kalmykia in Russia. The Mongols are the principal member of the large family of Mongolic peoples.

objects.title: Globe
objects.description: Scale model of a celestial body
objects.extract: A globe is a spherical model of Earth, of some other celestial body, or of the celestial sphere. Globes serve purposes similar to maps, but, unlike maps, they do not distort the surface that they portray except to scale it down.

objects.title: Firefighter
objects.description: Rescuer trained to extinguish fires and save people
objects.extract: A firefighter (or fire fighter or fireman) is a first responder trained in public safety and emergency response such as firefighting, primarily to control and extinguish fires and respond to emergencies such as hazardous material incidents, medical emergencies, road traffic collisions and other emergencies that threaten life, property and the environment, as

objects.title: Miles & Atwood Special
objects.extract: The Miles & Atwood Special is a racing aircraft developed during the interwar period
objects.artist: Alan Wilson from Stilton, Peterborough, Cambs, UK

objects.title: Peach
objects.description: Type of fruit tree, or its fruit
objects.extract: The peach (Prunus persica) is a deciduous tree that bears edible juicy fruits with various characteristics. Most are simply called peaches, while the glossy-skinned, non-fuzzy varieties are called nectarines.

objects.title: Airship
objects.extract: An airship, dirigible balloon or dirigible is a type of aerostat (lighter-than-air) aircraft that can navigate through the air flying under its own power. Aerostats use buoyancy from a lifting gas that is less dense than the surrounding air to achieve the lift needed to stay airborne.
objects.artist: Cliff from I now live in Arlington, VA (Outside Washington DC), USA

objects.title: Paddle steamer
objects.description: Steam-powered vessel propelled by paddle wheels
objects.extract: A paddle steamer is a steamship or steamboat powered by a steam engine driving paddle wheels to propel the craft through the water. Earlier ships that are driven by a paddle wheel under manual power are known as paddle wheelers.

objects.title: Headscarf
objects.description: Piece of cloth worn on one's head
objects.extract: A headscarf is a scarf covering most or all of the top of a person's, usually women's, hair and head, leaving the face uncovered. A headscarf is formed of a triangular cloth or a square cloth folded into a triangle, with which the head is covered.

objects.title: Laurel wreath
objects.description: Wreath made of branches and leaves of the bay laurel
objects.extract: A laurel wreath is a symbol of triumph, a wreath made of connected branches and leaves of the bay laurel (Laurus nobilis), an aromatic broadleaf evergreen.  It was also later made from spineless butcher's broom (Ruscus hypoglossum) or cherry laurel (Prunus laurocerasus).

objects.title: Knob Creek
objects.extract: Knob Creek is an American brand of Kentucky straight bourbon whiskey produced by Suntory Global Spirits (a subsidiary of Suntory Holdings of Osaka, Japan) at the Jim Beam distillery in Clermont, Kentucky.  It is one of the four Jim Beam small batch bourbon brands targeted for the high-end liquor market.

objects.title: Diesel locomotive
objects.description: Locomotive powered by a diesel engine
objects.extract: A diesel locomotive is a type of railway locomotive in which the power source is a diesel engine. Several types of diesel locomotives have been developed, differing mainly in the means by which mechanical power is conveyed to the driving wheels.

objects.title: Ground-effect vehicle
objects.description: Special vehicle to fly in air just above sea or ground
objects.extract: A ground-effect vehicle (GEV,  wing-in-ground-effect (WIGE or WIG), ground-effect craft/machine (GEM), wingship, flarecraft, surface effect vehicle or ekranoplan Russian: экранопла́н – "screenglider") is a winged vehicle that makes use of the ground effect to reduce aerodynamic drag. The ground effect is the interaction between a moving wing and a stationary

objects.title: Macaw
objects.description: Group of birds in the parrot family
objects.extract: Macaws are a group of large New World parrots that are long-tailed and often colorful, in the tribe Arini. They are popular in aviculture or as companion parrots, although there are conservation concerns about several species in the wild.

objects.title: Darevskia lindholmi
objects.extract: The Crimean rock lizard (Darevskia lindholmi) is a lizard species in the genus Darevskia. It is endemic to Ukraine.

objects.title: Punch
objects.artist: Photographe : Michael Abril (Q107184279), pour le compte de l’Association québéc

objects.title: Xenon arc lamp
objects.description: Gas discharge lamp that produces intense white light
objects.extract: A xenon arc lamp is a highly specialized type of gas discharge lamp, an electric light that produces light by passing electricity through ionized xenon gas at high pressure. It produces a bright white light to simulate sunlight, with applications in movie projectors in theaters, in searchlights, and for specialized uses in industry and research.

objects.title: E.J. and E. Griffith Interlocking Tower
objects.extract: E.J. and E. Griffith Interlocking Tower is a historic interlocking tower located at Griffith, Indiana. It was built in 1924 by the Elgin, Joliet and Eastern Railway.

objects.title: Salt cellar
objects.extract: A salt cellar (also called a salt, salt-box) is an article of tableware for holding and dispensing salt. In British English, the term can be used for what in North American English are called salt shakers.

objects.title: FOS.219
objects.description: Palaeoniscum freieslebeni fossil from Fish collection of Louis Agassiz in Natural history museum in Neuchâtel, Switzerland

objects.title: Horse tack
objects.description: Equipment for use on domesticated horses
objects.extract: Tack is equipment or accessories equipped on horses and other equines in the course of their use as domesticated animals. This equipment includes such items as saddles, stirrups, bridles, halters, reins, bits, and harnesses.

objects.title: Beer in South Korea
objects.extract: Beer, called maekju (Korean: 맥주; Hanja: 麥酒) in Korean, was first introduced to Korea in the early 20th century. Seoul's first brewery opened in 1908.

objects.title: Playing card
objects.description: Card used for playing various card games
objects.extract: A playing card is a piece of specially prepared card stock, heavy paper, thin cardboard, plastic-coated paper, cotton-paper blend, or thin plastic that is marked with distinguishing motifs. Often the front (face) and back of each card has a finish to make handling easier.

objects.title: Barrel
objects.extract: A barrel or cask is a hollow cylindrical container with a bulging center, longer than it is wide. They are traditionally made of wooden staves and bound by wooden or metal hoops.

objects.title: Aenetus virescens
objects.extract: The pūriri moth (Aenetus virescens), also commonly called the ghost moth or pepetuna, is a species of moth of the family Hepialidae. This moth is endemic to the North Island of New Zealand.
objects.artist: Birgit E. Rhode, Landcare Research New Zealand Ltd.

objects.title: Helitack
objects.description: Helicopter used to transport firefighters to fire zones
objects.extract: Helitack crews are teams of wildland firefighters who are transported by helicopter to wildfires. Helicopters provide rapid transport, enabling helitack crews to quickly respond and assess a wildfire situation.
objects.artist: Photo by: JO1 (SW/AW) STEPHEN WOOLVERTON, U.S. NAVY PHOTOGRAPHER

objects.title: Plastic bag
objects.extract: A plastic bag, poly bag,  or pouch is a type of container made of thin, flexible, plastic film, nonwoven fabric, or plastic textile. Plastic bags are used for containing and transporting goods such as foods, produce, powders, ice, magazines, chemicals, and waste.

objects.title: Ship's wheel
objects.extract: A ship's wheel or boat's wheel is a device used aboard a ship, boat, submarine, or airship, with which a helmsman steers the vessel and controls its course. Together with the rest of the steering mechanism, it forms part of the helm (the term helm can mean the wheel alone, or the entire mechanism by which the rudder is controlled).
objects.artist: U.S. Navy photo by Chief Photographer's Mate Don Bray

objects.title: Grasshopper
objects.description: Common name for a group of insects
objects.extract: Grasshoppers are a group of insects belonging to the suborder Caelifera. They are amongst what are possibly the most ancient living groups of chewing herbivorous insects, dating back to the early Triassic, around 250 million years ago.

objects.title: Ascot tie
objects.extract: An ascot tie, or simply ascot, is an article of neckwear with wide pointed wings at the end, wrapped around the neck and draped down the chest to cover the front placket and button line of a dress shirt. This wider relative of the necktie, an ascot tie is folded over, and usually fastened with an ascot tie pin, tie clip, or an ascot ring to secure the folds
objects.artist:
India_soldier.jpg: Still Thinking
derivative work: Jovianeye (talk)

objects.title: Space Shuttle
objects.description: Partially reusable launch system and space plane
objects.extract: The Space Shuttle is a retired, partially reusable low Earth orbital spacecraft system operated from 1981 to 2011 by the U.S. National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) as part of the Space Shuttle program. Its official program name was the Space Transportation System (STS), taken from the 1969 plan led by U.S. vice president Spiro Agnew for a syst

objects.title: Balkenbrij
objects.extract: Balkenbrij (also called 'karboet', 'tuet', or 'pannas') is a traditional Dutch food that shares some of the characteristics of American scrapple. Traditionally, its preparation and consumption were an important economizing custom, especially for the rural poor.

objects.title: Royal Nine-Tiered Umbrella
objects.description: Royal umbrella as symbol of a fully crowned king in Thailand
objects.extract: The Royal Nine-Tiered Umbrella (Thai: นพปฎลมหาเศวตฉัตร: Nopphapadon Mahasawettachat, officially called the Nine-Tiered Great White Umbrella of State) is considered the most sacred and ancient of the royal regalia of Thailand. A royal umbrella (also called a chatra) consists of many tiers, five for the prince, seven for the crown prince (or the viceroy) and u

objects.title: Tuatara
objects.extract: The tuatara (, Māori: [ˈtʉ.a.ta.ɾa]; Sphenodon punctatus) is a species of reptile endemic to New Zealand. Despite its close resemblance to lizards, it is the only extant member of a distinct lineage, the previously highly diverse order Rhynchocephalia.

objects.title: Apple Watch Ultra
objects.extract: The Apple Watch Ultra is a line of sport smartwatches produced by Apple Inc. as part of the Apple Watch product line.

objects.title: Woodturning
objects.extract: Woodturning is the craft of using a wood lathe with hand-held tools to cut a shape that is symmetrical around the axis of rotation. Like the potter's wheel, the wood lathe is a mechanism that can generate a variety of forms.

objects.title: Xiahe mandible
objects.extract: The Xiahe mandible ([ɕjâxɤ̌], sh'ya-khuh) is a hominin fossil jaw (mandible) discovered in Baishiya Karst Cave, located on the northeastern edge of the Tibetan Plateau in Xiahe County, Gansu, China. By the use of palaeoproteomic analysis, it is the first confirmed discovery of a Denisovan fossil outside of Denisova Cave, and was the most complete confirmed D

objects.title: Ice skate
objects.description: Boots with blades attached to the bottom for propelling the bearer across a sheet of ice
objects.extract:
Ice skates are metal blades attached underfoot and used to propel the bearer across a sheet of ice while ice skating.
The first ice skates were made from leg bones of horse, ox or deer, and were attached to feet with leather straps.

objects.title: Recumbent bicycle
objects.extract: A recumbent bicycle is a bicycle that places the rider in a laid-back reclining position, and often called a human-powered vehicle or HPV, especially if it has an aerodynamic fairing. Recumbents are available in a wide range of configurations, including: long to short wheelbase; large, small, or a mix of wheel sizes; overseat, underseat, or no-hands steering

objects.title: Aglyptodactylus laticeps
objects.extract:
Aglyptodactylus laticeps is a species of frog in the family Mantellidae.
It is endemic to Madagascar.

objects.title: Stained glass
objects.description: Colored glass and works that are made from it
objects.extract: Stained glass refers to coloured glass as a material or art and architectural works created from it. Although it is traditionally made in flat panels and used as windows, the creations of modern stained glass artists also include three-dimensional structures and sculpture.

objects.title: Snowcat
objects.description: Enclosed-cab, truck-sized, fully tracked vehicle designed for over-snow travel
objects.extract: A snowcat (a portmanteau of snow and caterpillar) is an enclosed-cab, truck-sized, fully tracked vehicle designed to travel over snow. Major manufacturers are PistenBully (Germany), Prinoth (Italy), and Tucker (United States).

objects.title: List of drinks
objects.extract: Drinks are liquids that can be consumed, with drinking water being the base ingredient for many of them. In addition to basic needs, drinks form part of the culture of human society.

objects.title: Noh
objects.extract: Noh (能, Nō; Japanese pronunciation: [no(ꜜ)ː], Sino-Japanese for "ability") is a major form of classical Japanese dance-drama that has been performed since the 14th century. It is Japan's oldest major theater art that is still regularly performed today.

objects.title: Samosa
objects.extract: A samosa () () is a fried Indian subcontinent turnover with a savoury filling that mostly consists of vegetables like spiced potatoes, onions, and peas, but can include cheese such as paneer, meat or even fish. Its name originates from the Middle Persian word sambosag (سنبوسگ) (meaning 'triangular pastry').

objects.title: Artillery tractor
objects.description: Specialized heavy-duty form of tractor unit used to tow artillery pieces
objects.extract: An artillery tractor, also referred to as a gun tractor, is a specialized heavy-duty form of tractor unit used to tow artillery pieces of varying weights and calibres.  It may be wheeled, tracked, or half-tracked.

objects.title: Cornu (horn)
objects.extract: A cornu or cornum (Latin: cornū, cornūs or cornum, "horn", sometimes translated misleadingly as "cornet"; pl.: cornua) was an ancient Roman brass instrument about 3 m (9.8 ft) long in the shape of a letter 'G'. The instrument was braced by a crossbar that stiffened the structure and provided a means of supporting its weight on the player's shoulder.

objects.title: Sheep
objects.description: Domesticated ruminant bred for meat, wool, and milk
objects.extract: Sheep (pl.: sheep) or domestic sheep  (Ovis aries) are a domesticated, ruminant mammal typically kept as livestock. Although the term sheep can apply to other species in the genus Ovis, in everyday usage it almost always refers to domesticated sheep.

objects.title: Screwdriver
objects.description: Hand tool used for turning screws
objects.extract: A  screwdriver is a tool, manual or powered, used for turning screws.

objects.title: Vacuum tube
objects.description: Device that controls current between electrodes
objects.extract: A vacuum tube, electron tube, thermionic valve (British usage), or tube (North America) is a device that controls electric current flow in a high vacuum between electrodes to which an electric potential difference has been applied. It takes the form of an evacuated tubular envelope of glass or sometimes metal containing electrodes connected to external conne

objects.title: Automotive lighting
objects.description: Lighting system of a motor vehicle
objects.extract: Automotive lighting is functional exterior lighting in vehicles. A motor vehicle has lighting and signaling devices mounted to or integrated into its front, rear, sides, and, in some cases, top.

objects.title: Fry's Electronics
objects.description: Former American big-box store electronics retailer
objects.extract: Fry's Electronics was an American big-box store chain. It was headquartered in San Jose, California, in Silicon Valley.

objects.title: Cuberdon
objects.extract: A cuberdon is a cone-shaped Belgian candy and a popular local delicacy in Ghent. In Dutch it is also known as a neus ("nose"), Gentse neus ("Ghent nose"), or neuzeke ("little nose") for its likeness to a human nose.

objects.title: Monster High
objects.artist: The Conmunity -  Pop Culture Geek from Los Angeles, CA, USA

objects.title: Cat
objects.extract: The cat (Felis catus), also called domestic cat and house cat, is a small domesticated carnivorous mammal. It is an obligate carnivore, requiring a predominantly meat-based diet.

objects.title: Freddy Parent
objects.extract: Alfred Joseph Parent (November 11, 1875 – November 2, 1972) was an American professional baseball player. He played all or part of eleven seasons in Major League Baseball (MLB), between 1899 and 1911, for the St.

objects.title: Bone china
objects.description: Porcelain composed of bone ash, feldspathic material, and kaolin
objects.extract: Bone china is a type of vitreous, translucent pottery, the raw materials for which include bone ash, feldspathic material and kaolin. It has been defined as "ware with a translucent body" containing a minimum of 30% of phosphate derived from calcined animal bone or calcium phosphate.

objects.title: Mosbolletjies
objects.extract: Mosbolletjies is a traditional Afrikaner or Cape Dutch sweet-bun or bread traditionally made in the wine producing areas of the Western Cape province of South Africa. The name is Afrikaans in origin and is a combination of mos (Afrikaans for partially fermented grape juice) and bolletjies (Afrikaans for "balls" or "buns").

objects.title: Felt
objects.extract: Felt is a textile that is produced by matting, condensing, and pressing fibers together. Felt can be made of natural fibers such as wool or animal fur, or from synthetic fibers such as petroleum-based acrylic or acrylonitrile or wood pulp–based rayon.

objects.title: Vincent van Gogh
objects.extract: Vincent Willem van Gogh (Dutch: [ˈvɪnsɛnt ˈʋɪləɱ vɑŋ ˈɣɔx] ; 30 March 1853 – 29 July 1890) was a Dutch Post-Impressionist painter who is among the most famous and influential figures in the history of Western art. In just over a decade he created about 2,100 artworks, including around 860 oil paintings, most of them in the last two years of his life.

objects.title: Roller skating
objects.description: Sport, activity, or form of transportation
objects.extract: Roller skating is the act of travelling on surfaces with roller skates. It is a recreational activity, a sport, and a form of transportation.

objects.title: Pierogi
objects.extract: Pierogi ( pirr-OH-ghee; Polish: [pjɛˈrɔɡʲi] , sing. pieróg [ˈpjɛruk] ) are filled dumplings made by wrapping unleavened dough around a filling and cooking in boiling water.

objects.title: Unicycle
objects.extract: A unicycle, also monocycle is a vehicle that touches the ground with only one wheel. The most common variation has a frame with a saddle, and has a pedal-driven direct-drive.

objects.title: Model car
objects.extract: A model car, or toy car, is a miniature representation of an automobile. Other miniature motor vehicles, such as trucks, buses, and ATVs, etc.

objects.title: Aesthetics
objects.artist: White House photo by Eric Draper

objects.title: Nydam Boat
objects.description: Iron Age boat found at Nydam Mose, Denmark

objects.title: Alexander the Great
objects.description: King of Macedon from 336 to 323 BC
objects.extract: Alexander III of Macedon (Ancient Greek: Ἀλέξανδρος, romanized: Aléxandros; 20/21 July 356 BC – 10/11 June 323 BC), most commonly known as Alexander the Great, was king of the ancient Greek kingdom of Macedon. He succeeded his father Philip II to the throne in 336 BC at the age of 20, and spent most of his reign conducting a lengthy military campaign through

objects.title: Laptop
objects.extract: A laptop is a portable personal computer (PC). Laptops typically have a clamshell form factor with a flat-panel screen on the inside of the upper lid and an alphanumeric keyboard and pointing device on the inside of the lower lid.

objects.title: Banjara Hound
objects.extract: The Banjara Hound, also known as the Banjari Hound, is a breed of dog found in India. It is a sighthound-type dog bred and used for hunting by the nomadic Banjara of Maharashtra.
objects.artist: not specified (except those with signature on image)

objects.title: Roller speed skating at the 2018 Summer Youth Olympics – Boys 500m Sprint Quarterfinals Run 2
Pomelo
objects.extract: The pomelo ( POM-il-oh, PUM-; or pummelo, Citrus maxima), also known as a shaddock, is the largest citrus fruit. It is an ancestor of several cultivated citrus species, including the bitter orange and the grapefruit.

objects.title: Louis XVI style
objects.description: Neoclassical style within architecture and design
objects.extract: Louis XVI style, also called Louis Seize, is a style of architecture, furniture, decoration and art which developed in France during the 18-year reign of Louis XVI (1774–1792), just before the French Revolution. It saw the final phase of the Baroque style as well as the birth of French Neoclassicism.

objects.title: Self-contained breathing apparatus
objects.description: Breathing gas supplied respirator carried by the user
objects.extract: A self-contained breathing apparatus (SCBA) is a respirator worn to provide an autonomous supply of breathable gas in an atmosphere that is immediately dangerous to life or health from a gas cylinder. They are typically used in firefighting and industry.

objects.title: Denim skirt
objects.extract: A denim skirt, sometimes referred to as a 'jean skirt' or 'jeans skirt', is a skirt made of denim, the same material as blue jeans. Denim skirts come in a variety of styles and lengths to suit different populations and occasions.

objects.title: William Brewster (Mayflower passenger)
objects.extract: William Brewster (c. 1566/67 – 10 April 1644) was an English official and Mayflower passenger in 1620.

objects.title: Mace-AO 21368
objects.description: bronze mace with two rows of cuneiform writings

objects.title: Prairie dog
objects.extract: Prairie dogs (genus Cynomys) are herbivorous burrowing ground squirrels native to the grasslands of North America. There are five recognized species of prairie dog: black-tailed, white-tailed, Gunnison's, Utah, and Mexican prairie dogs.

objects.title: Hanukkah
objects.extract:
Hanukkah (IPA: ; Hebrew: חֲנֻכָּה, romanized: Ḥănukkā, lit. 'dedication', ) is a Jewish holiday that commemorates the Maccabean Revolt against the Seleucid Empire in the 2nd century BCE, when the Maccabees successfully recovered Jerusalem and the Second Temple.
Beginning on the 25th of Kislev on the Hebrew calendar, Hanukkah lasts for eight nights and days.

objects.title: Tricholoma virgatum
objects.extract: Tricholoma virgatum, commonly known as the ashen knight, is a mushroom of the agaric genus Tricholoma.
objects.artist: This image was created by user Ron Pastorino (Ronpast) at Mushroom Observer, a s

objects.title: Cadbury
objects.extract: Cadbury (formerly Cadbury's and Cadbury Schweppes) is a British multinational confectionery company owned by Mondelez International (spun off from Kraft Foods) since 2010. It is the second-largest confectionery brand in the world, after Mars.

objects.title: Puppetry
objects.description: Form of theatre or performance that involves the manipulation of puppets
objects.extract: Puppetry is a form of theatre or performance that involves the manipulation of puppets, the inanimate objects, often resembling some type of human or animal figure, that are animated or manipulated by a human called a puppeteer. Such a performance is also known as a puppet production.

objects.title: List of Singaporean dishes
objects.extract:
This is a list of Singaporean dishes.
Singaporean cuisine includes both unique dishes and others that, while sharing names with dishes in other cuisines, have evolved to mean something distinctly different in Singapore.

objects.title: Cooking
objects.extract: Cooking, also known as cookery, is the art, science and craft of using heat to make food more palatable, digestible, nutritious, or safe. Cooking techniques and ingredients vary widely, from grilling food over an open fire, to using electric stoves, to baking in various types of ovens, to boiling and blanching in water, reflecting local conditions, technique

objects.title: Pelargonium
objects.extract: Pelargonium () is a genus of flowering plants commonly called geraniums, pelargoniums, or storksbills. It includes about 280 species of perennials, succulents, and shrubs.

objects.title: 559 series integrated circuits
objects.description: series of Soviet TTL logic circuits

objects.title: Truck
objects.extract: A truck (North American and Australian English) or lorry (British English) is a motor vehicle designed to transport freight, carry specialized payloads, or perform other utilitarian work. Trucks vary greatly in size, power, and configuration, but the vast majority feature body-on-frame construction, with a cabin that is independent of the payload portion of

objects.title: Bookcase
objects.extract: A bookcase also called a bookshelf, is a piece of furniture with horizontal shelves, often in a cabinet, used to store books or other printed materials. Bookcases are used in private homes, public and university libraries, offices, schools, and bookstores.
objects.artist: Craig Conley from Durham, NC, US

objects.title: Laser tag
objects.extract: Laser tag is a recreational shooting sport where participants use infrared-emitting light guns to tag designated targets. Infrared-sensitive signaling devices are commonly worn by each player to register hits and are sometimes integrated within the arena in which the game is played.

objects.title: Beau Sancy
objects.description: Historic 35 carat diamond of faint brown color
objects.extract: The Beau Sancy is a 34.98-carat (6.996 g) diamond originally mined in India, which is cut in a modified pear double rose-cut shape. Since appearing in Europe in the 16th century, the Beau Sancy has been owned by a number of European royal houses, among them the House of Medici and the kings of England and Prussia.

objects.title: Furniture
objects.description: Objects used to support human activities
objects.extract: Furniture refers to objects intended to support various human activities, such as seating (e.g., stools, chairs, and sofas), eating (tables), storing items, working, and sleeping (e.g., beds and hammocks). Furniture is also used to hold objects at a convenient height for work (as horizontal surfaces above the ground, such as tables and desks), or to store th

objects.title: Almond milk
objects.extract: Almond milk is a plant-based milk substitute with a watery texture and nutty flavor manufactured from almonds, although some types or brands are flavored in imitation of cow's milk. It does not contain cholesterol or lactose and is low in saturated fat.

objects.title: Mongoose
objects.description: Family of mammals in Africa and Asia
objects.extract: A mongoose is a small terrestrial carnivorous mammal belonging to the family Herpestidae. This family has two subfamilies, the Herpestinae and the Mungotinae.

objects.title: Water tower
objects.extract: A water tower is an elevated structure supporting a water tank constructed at a height sufficient to pressurize a distribution system for potable water, and to provide emergency storage for fire protection. Water towers often operate in conjunction with underground or surface service reservoirs, which store treated water close to where it will be used.

objects.title: Succulent plant
objects.extract:
In botany, succulent plants, also known as succulents, are plants with parts that are thickened, fleshy, and engorged, usually to retain water in arid climates or soil conditions.
Succulents may store water in various structures, such as leaves and stems.

objects.title: Darda
objects.extract:
Darda is the name of a German toy car racing set (and related items) which was most popular in Europe and the USA throughout the 1980s and '90s.
The unique selling point of the sets is the special Darda Motor, invented by Helmut Darda in 1970, which propelled the cars (similarly sized to Matchbox or Hot Wheels) at speeds of up to 30 mph (50 km/h).

objects.title: 190 series integrated circuits
objects.description: Soviet series of MOS switch integrated circuits

objects.title: Celery
objects.extract: Celery (Apium graveolens Dulce Group or Apium graveolens var. dulce) is a cultivated plant belonging to the species Apium graveolens in the family Apiaceae that has been used as a vegetable since ancient times.
objects.artist:
fir0002  flagstaffotos [at] gmail.com
		Canon 20D + Tamron 28-75mm f/2.8

objects.title: Mime artist
objects.description: Person who uses mime as a theatrical medium or performance art
objects.extract: A mime artist, or simply mime (from Greek μῖμος, mimos, "imitator, actor"), is a person who uses mime (also called pantomime outside of Britain), the acting out of a story through body motions without the use of speech, as a theatrical medium or as a performance art. In earlier times, in English, such a performer would typically be referred to as a mummer.

objects.title: Ophthalmology
objects.description: Field of medicine treating eye disorders
objects.extract: Ophthalmology (, OFF-thal-MOL-ə-jee) is the branch of medicine that deals with the diagnosis, treatment, and surgery of eye diseases and disorders. Ophthalmologists also diagnose systemic (other parts of the body) diseases using changes seen in the eyes.

objects.title: Mongoose
objects.description: Family of mammals in Africa and Asia
objects.extract: A mongoose is a small terrestrial carnivorous mammal belonging to the family Herpestidae. This family has two subfamilies, the Herpestinae and the Mungotinae.
objects.artist: Taken by Schuyler Shepherd (Unununium272)Canon 350D, 100-400mm f/4.5-5.6 L IS.

objects.title: SS Emerald
objects.extract: SS The Emerald was a cruise ship owned by Louis Cruise Lines (now Celestyal Cruises). She was built in 1958 by the Newport News Shipbuilding and Drydock company in Newport News, Virginia, United States, for the Grace Line, as the ocean liner Santa Rosa.

objects.title: Neocaridina fonticulata
objects.artist: Shih H-T, Cai Y, Chiu Y-W (2019) Neocaridina fonticulata, a new land-locked fres

objects.title: Electrical ballast
objects.description: Device to limit the current in lamps
objects.extract:
An electrical ballast is a device placed in series with a load to limit the amount of current in an  electrical circuit.
A familiar and widely used example is the inductive ballast used in fluorescent lamps to limit the current through the tube, which would otherwise rise to a destructive level due to the negative differential resistance of the tube's voltag

objects.title: Teaware
objects.description: Traditional equipment for drinking tea in some cultures
objects.extract: Teaware is a broad international spectrum of equipment used in the brewing and consumption of tea. Many components make up that spectrum, and vary greatly based upon the type of tea being prepared, and the cultural setting in which it is being prepared.

objects.title: Lantern
objects.extract: A lantern is a source of lighting, often portable. It typically features a protective enclosure for the light source – historically usually a candle, a wick in oil, or a heated mesh, and often a battery-powered light in modern times – to make it easier to carry and hang up, and make it more reliable outdoors or in drafty interiors.

objects.title: Bird
objects.description: Warm-blooded animals with wings and feathers
objects.extract: Birds are a group of warm-blooded vertebrate animals constituting the class Aves, characterised by feathers, toothless beaked jaws, the laying of hard-shelled eggs, a high metabolic rate, a four-chambered heart, and a strong yet lightweight skeleton. Birds live worldwide and range in size from the 5.5 cm (2.2 in) bee hummingbird to the 2.8 m (9 ft 2 in) comm

objects.title: Lacquerware
objects.extract: Lacquerware are objects decoratively covered with lacquer. Lacquerware includes small or large containers, tableware, miscellaneous lacquered small objects carried by people, and larger objects such as furniture and even coffins painted with lacquer.

objects.title: Paralouatta varonai
objects.description: Extinct genus of new world monkeys
objects.extract: Paralouatta is a platyrrhine genus that currently contains two extinct species of small primates that lived on the island of Cuba.

objects.title: Koi
objects.extract:
Koi (Japanese: 鯉; Japanese: [koꜜi], literally 'carp'), or more specifically nishikigoi (錦鯉; Japanese: [ɲiɕi̥kiꜜɡoi], literally 'brocaded carp'), are colored varieties of carp (Cyprinus sp.) that are kept for decorative purposes in outdoor koi ponds or water gardens. 
Koi is an informal name for the colored variants of carp kept for ornamental purposes.
objects.artist:
Maxikoi at Dutch Wikipedia
Later version(s) were uploaded by Erik Baas at nl.wik

objects.title: Bladder fiddle
objects.extract: The bladder fiddle was a folk instrument used throughout Europe and in the Americas. The instrument was originally a simple large stringed fiddle (a musical bow) made with a long stick, one or more thick gut strings, and a pig's-bladder resonator.

objects.title: Trousers
objects.description: Clothing for the legs and lower body
objects.extract:
Trousers (British English), slacks, or pants (Northern, American, Canadian, and Australian English) are an item of clothing worn from the waist to anywhere between the knees and the ankles, covering both legs separately (rather than with cloth extending across both legs as in robes, skirts, dresses, and kilts).
Shorts are similar to trousers, but with legs t

objects.title: Censer
objects.description: Vessel for burning incense or perfume
objects.extract: A censer, incense burner, perfume burner or pastille burner is a vessel made for burning incense or perfume in some solid form. They vary greatly in size, form, and material of construction, and have been in use since ancient times throughout the world.

objects.title: Hammer
objects.extract: A  hammer is a tool, most often a hand tool, consisting of a weighted "head" fixed to a long handle that is swung to deliver an impact to a small area of an object. This can be, for example, to drive nails into wood, to shape metal (as with a forge), or to crush rock.

objects.title: Mosaic
objects.description: Image made from small colored tiles
objects.extract: A mosaic () is a pattern or image made of small regular or irregular pieces of colored stone, glass or ceramic, held in place by plaster/mortar, and covering a surface. Mosaics are often used as floor and wall decoration, and were particularly popular in the Ancient Roman world.

objects.title: Wardrobe (Kast)
objects.description: furniture and woodwork by unknown artist (1982.57)

objects.title: Paver (vehicle)
objects.description: Construction equipment used to lay asphalt
objects.extract: A paver (road paver finisher, asphalt finisher, road paving machine) is a piece of construction equipment used to lay asphalt concrete or Portland cement concrete on roads, bridges, parking lots and other such places. It lays the material flat and provides minor compaction.

objects.title: Traffic collision
objects.description: Incident when a vehicle collides with another object
objects.extract: A traffic collision, also known as a motor vehicle collision or car crash, occurs when a vehicle collides with another vehicle, pedestrian, animal, road debris, or other moving or stationary obstruction, such as a tree, boulder, pole, or structure. Traffic collisions often result in injury, disability, death, and property damage as well as financial costs to

objects.title: Aquamarine-MGL 51051
objects.description: sample of aquamarine found at Dusso, Karakoram, Pakistan

objects.title: Sperm whale
objects.extract: The sperm whale or cachalot (Physeter macrocephalus) is the largest of the toothed whales and the largest toothed predator. It is the only living member of the genus Physeter and one of three extant species in the sperm whale superfamily Physeteroidea, along with the pygmy sperm whale and dwarf sperm whale of the genus Kogia.

objects.title: Turnover (food)
objects.description: Pastry with a filling on a single piece of dough which has been folded over and sealed
objects.extract: A turnover is a baked or fried good, made by placing a filling on a piece of pastry or viennoiserie dough. The dough is then folded over and sealed, and is then cooked by either baking or frying.

objects.title: Oratank
objects.description: small oil/chemical tanker ship built in 2008

objects.title: Clavus with plaited decoration and images of a saint with a halo
objects.description: woolen and linen clavus of the Byzantine Egypt, 600–800 CE, in the collection of Museo Egizio, Turin, Italy (S.1775/= 17419)

objects.title: Victorian decorative arts
objects.extract: Victorian decorative arts are the style of decorative arts during the Victorian era. Victorian design is widely viewed as having indulged in a grand excess of ornament.

objects.title: Snifter
objects.description: Short-stemmed glass with a wide bottom and a narrow top
objects.extract: A snifter (also called brandy balloon, brandy snifter, brandy glass, brandy bowl or a cognac glass) is a type of stemware, a short-stemmed glass whose vessel has a wide bottom and a relatively narrow top. It is mostly used to serve aged brown liquors such as bourbon, brandy and whisky.

objects.title: Harvester (forestry)
objects.description: Type of forestry vehicle employed in logging operations
objects.extract: A harvester is a type of heavy forestry vehicle employed in cut-to-length logging operations for felling, delimbing and bucking trees. A forest harvester is typically employed together with a skidder that hauls the logs to a roadside landing, or a forwarder to pick up and haul away.

objects.title: Lever
objects.description: Simple machine consisting of a beam pivoted at a fixed hinge
objects.extract: A lever is a simple machine consisting of a beam or rigid rod pivoted at a fixed hinge, or fulcrum. A lever is a rigid body capable of rotating on a point on itself.

objects.title: Seaplane
objects.description: Aircraft with an undercarriage capable of operating from water surfaces
objects.extract: A seaplane is a powered fixed-wing aircraft capable of taking off and landing (alighting) on water. Seaplanes are usually divided into two categories based on their technological characteristics: floatplanes and flying boats; the latter are generally far larger and can carry far more.

objects.title: Jacky Winter
objects.extract: The jacky winter (Microeca fascinans) is a small grey-brown robin found commonly throughout Australia and also in Papua New Guinea. The jacky winter acquired its name due to rapid and strong vocalisations, which sound like jacky-jacky winter-winter.
objects.artist:
fir0002  flagstaffotos [at] gmail.com
		Canon 20D + Canon 400mm f/5.6 L

objects.title: Seafood
objects.description: Marine life regarded as food by humans
objects.extract: Seafood is any form of sea life regarded as food by humans, prominently including fish and shellfish. Shellfish include various species of molluscs (e.g., bivalve molluscs such as clams, oysters, and mussels, and cephalopods such as octopus and squid), crustaceans (e.g.

objects.title: Goblet
objects.description: goblet at the Metropolitan Museum of Art (MET, 1971.153.4)

objects.title: Glass milk bottle
objects.extract: From the second half of the 19th century, milk has been packaged and delivered in reusable and returnable glass bottles. They are used mainly for doorstep delivery of fresh milk by milkmen.

objects.title: San Diego Zoo
objects.description: Zoo in San Diego, California, U.S
objects.extract: The San Diego Zoo is a zoo in San Diego, California, United States, located in Balboa Park. It began with a collection of animals left over from the 1915 Panama–California Exposition that were brought together by its founder, Dr.

objects.title: Theatre
objects.extract: Theatre or theater is a collaborative form of performing art that uses live performers, usually actors, to present experiences of a real or imagined event before a live audience in a specific place, often a stage. The performers may communicate this experience to the audience through combinations of gesture, speech, song, music, and dance.

objects.title: Fishing reel
objects.description: Hand-cranked reel used in angling to stow fishing line
objects.extract:
A fishing reel is a hand-cranked reel used in angling to wind and stow fishing line, typically mounted onto a fishing rod, but may also be used on compound bows or crossbows to retrieve tethered arrows when bowfishing.
Modern recreational fishing reels usually have fittings aiding in casting for distance and accuracy, as well as controlling the speed and ten

objects.title: Oboe
objects.extract: The oboe ( OH-boh) is a type of double-reed woodwind instrument. Oboes are usually made of wood, but may also be made of synthetic materials, such as plastic, resin, or hybrid composites.

objects.title: Marco Polo
objects.extract: Marco Polo (c. 1254 –  8 January 1324) was a Venetian merchant, explorer, and writer who travelled through Asia along the Silk Road between 1271 and 1295. His travels are recorded in The Travels of Marco Polo (also known as Book of the Marvels of the World  and Il Milione, c. 1300), a book that described the then-mysterious culture and inner workings of the

objects.title: Parable of the Prodigal Son
objects.description: Parable from the Gospel of Luke
objects.extract: The parable of the Prodigal Son (also known as the parable of the Two Brothers, Lost Son, Loving Father, or of the Forgiving Father; Greek: Παραβολή του Ασώτου Υιού, romanized: Parabolē tou Asōtou Huiou) is one of the parables of Jesus in the Bible, appearing in Luke 15:11–32. In Luke 15, Jesus tells this story, along with those of a man with 100 sheep and a

objects.title: Coffea
objects.extract: Coffea is a genus of flowering plants in the family Rubiaceae. Coffea species are shrubs or small trees native to tropical and southern Africa and tropical Asia.

objects.title: Rainstick
objects.extract: A rainstick is a long, hollow tube of bamboo or dried cactus that is partially flooded with small pebbles, rice, dried beans, or other hard granular matter. The inside surface of the tube has small pins or thorns arranged helically.

objects.title: Christianization
objects.description: Conversion of society or culture to Christianity
objects.extract: Christianization (or Christianisation) is a term for the specific type of change that occurs when someone or something has been or is being converted to Christianity. Christianization has, for the most part, spread through missions by individual conversions, but has also been the result of violence.

objects.title: Hand fan
objects.extract: A handheld fan, or simply hand fan, is a broad, flat surface that is waved back and forth to create an airflow. Generally, purpose-made handheld fans are folding fans, which are shaped like a sector of a circle and made of a thin material (such as paper or feathers) mounted on slats which revolve around a pivot so that it can be closed when not in use.
objects.artist: PFC Kim Jun-sub, 8th U.S. Army Public Affairs

objects.title: Caulk
objects.description: Flexible material used in construction to seal joints
objects.extract: Caulk (also known as caulking and calking) is a material used to seal joints or seams against leakage in various structures and piping. The same word is also the verb for the action of applying such sealing material.

objects.title: Bee
objects.extract: Bees are winged insects that form a monophyletic clade Anthophila within the superfamily Apoidea of the order Hymenoptera, with over 20,000 known species in seven recognized families. Some species – including honey bees, bumblebees, and stingless bees – are social insects living in highly hierarchical colonies, while over 90% of bee species – including mason

objects.title: Goldfish
objects.extract: The goldfish (Carassius auratus) is a freshwater fish in the family Cyprinidae of the order Cypriniformes. It is commonly kept as a pet in indoor aquariums, and is one of the most popular aquarium fish.

objects.title: John A. Wilson (sculptor)
objects.extract: John Albert Wilson (1877 – December 8, 1954) was a Canadian sculptor who produced public art for commissions throughout North America. He was a professor in the School of Architecture at Harvard University for 32 years.

objects.title: Bordeaux wine
objects.description: Wine produced in the Bordeaux region of France
objects.extract: Bordeaux wine (Occitan: vin de Bordèu; French: vin de Bordeaux) is produced in the Bordeaux region of southwest France, around the city of Bordeaux, on the Garonne River. To the north of the city, the Dordogne River joins the Garonne forming the broad estuary called the Gironde; the Gironde department, with a total vineyard area of 110,800 hectares, is the s

objects.title: Europa Imaging System
objects.description: visible spectrum wide and narrow angle camera on the Europa Clipper spacecraft

objects.title: Tap (valve)
objects.description: Valve controlling the release of a liquid or gas
objects.extract: A tap (also spigot or faucet: see usage variations) is a valve controlling the release of a fluid.

objects.title: Kiko Mizuhara
objects.extract:
Audrie Kiko Daniel (born October 15, 1990), known professionally as Kiko Mizuhara (水原 希子, Mizuhara Kiko), is an American-born Japanese model, actress, singer and designer.
Kiko Mizuhara started her modeling career at the age of twelve when she entered an audition contest for Seventeen magazine in which readers selected their favorite new face for the publica

objects.title: Morus (plant)
objects.extract: Morus, a genus of flowering plants in the family Moraceae, consists of 19 species of deciduous trees commonly known as mulberries, growing wild and under cultivation in many temperate world regions. Generally, the genus has 64 subordinate taxa, though the three most common are referred to as white, red, and black, originating from the color of their dormant

objects.title: Hammock
objects.description: Sling used for swinging, sleeping or resting
objects.extract: A hammock, from Spanish hamaca, borrowed from Taíno and Arawak hamaka, is a sling made of fabric, rope, or netting, suspended between two or more points, used for swinging, sleeping, or resting. It normally consists of one or more cloth panels, or a woven network of twine or thin rope stretched with ropes between two firm anchor points such as trees or posts

objects.title: Camera phone
objects.extract: A camera phone is a mobile phone that is able to capture photographs and often record video using one or more built-in digital cameras. It can also send the resulting image wirelessly and conveniently.

objects.title: cümbüş
objects.extract: The cümbüş (; Turkish pronunciation: [dʒymˈbyʃ]) is a Turkish stringed instrument of relatively modern origin. It was developed in 1930 by Zeynel Abidin Cümbüş as an oud-like instrument that could be heard as part of a larger ensemble.

objects.title: Amber
objects.extract: Amber is fossilized tree resin. It has been appreciated for its color (orange, brown and, sometimes, red) and natural beauty since the Neolithic times, and worked as a gemstone since classical antiquity.

objects.title: Algae
objects.extract: Algae (  AL-jee, UK also  AL-ghee; sing.: alga  ) are any of a large and diverse group of photosynthetic organisms. It excludes the land plants (embryophytes).
objects.artist: Photo by NEON ja, colored by Richard Bartz

objects.title: Species
objects.description: Basic unit of taxonomic classification, below genus
objects.extract: A species (pl. species) is the basic unit of classification and a taxonomic rank of an organism, as well as a unit of biodiversity. It can be defined as the largest group of organisms in which any two individuals of the appropriate sexes or mating types can produce fertile offspring, typically by sexual reproduction.

objects.title: Polypterus weeksii
objects.extract: Polypterus weeksii, the mottled bichir, is a fish in the family Polypteridae found in the central basin of the Congo River.  It grows to about 54 cm in head-to-tail length.

objects.title: Yak
objects.extract: The yak (Bos grunniens), also known as the Tartary ox, grunting ox, hairy cattle, sarlak or sarlyk, or domestic yak, is a species of long-haired domesticated cattle found throughout the Himalayan region, the Tibetan Plateau, Tajikistan, the Pamir Mountains, and as far north as Mongolia and Siberia. It is descended from the wild yak (Bos mutus).

objects.title: Skeleton watch
objects.description: Mechanical watch with moving parts visible
objects.extract: A skeleton watch is a mechanical watch in which the moving parts are visible. This may be through either the front of the watch, the back of the watch or a small cut outlining the dial.

objects.title: Harrow (tool)
objects.extract: In agriculture, a harrow is a farm implement used for surface tillage. It is used after ploughing  for breaking up and smoothing out the surface of the soil.

objects.title: Combat boot
objects.description: Type of boots designed to be worn by soldiers
objects.extract: Combat or tactical boots are military boots designed to be worn by soldiers during combat or combat training, as opposed to during parades and other ceremonial duties. Modern combat boots are designed to provide a combination of grip, ankle stability, and foot protection suitable for a rugged environment.

objects.title: John Deere
objects.description: American agricultural and industrial auto manufacturing corporation
objects.extract: Deere & Company (doing business as John Deere) (), is an American corporation that manufactures agricultural machinery, heavy equipment, forestry machinery, diesel engines, drivetrains (axles, transmissions, gearboxes) used in heavy equipment and lawn care equipment. It also provides financial services and other related activities.

objects.title: Bumblebee
objects.extract: A bumblebee (or bumble bee, bumble-bee, or humble-bee) is any of over 250 species in the genus Bombus, part of Apidae, one of the bee families. This genus is the only extant group in the tribe Bombini, though a few extinct related genera (e.g., Calyptapis) are known from fossils.

objects.title: Windmill
objects.description: Machine that makes use of wind energy
objects.extract: A windmill is a machine operated by the force of wind acting on vanes or sails to mill grain (gristmills). Windmills were used throughout the high medieval and early modern periods; the horizontal or panemone windmill first appeared in Persia during the 9th century, and the vertical windmill first appeared in northwestern Europe in the 12th century.

objects.title: Salad
objects.description: Food mixture, served chilled or at room temperature
objects.extract: A salad is a dish consisting of mixed ingredients, frequently vegetables. They are typically served chilled or at room temperature, though some can be served warm.

objects.title: Paper clothing
objects.extract:
Paper clothing refers to garments and accessories made from paper or paper substitutes. 
The earliest known paper clothing was made by the Chinese even before they used paper as a writing medium in the 2nd century CE. Paper clothing, usually made from washi paper, was developed by the Chinese through the centuries, the craft spreading through Asia, until it

objects.title: Street light
objects.description: Raised source of light beside a road or path
objects.extract: A street light, light pole, lamp pole, lamppost, streetlamp, light standard, or lamp standard is a raised source of light on the edge of a road or path. Similar lights may be found on a railway platform.

objects.title: Plzeň
objects.extract: Plzeň (Czech pronunciation: [ˈpl̩zɛɲ] ), also known in English and German as Pilsen (German: [ˈpɪlzn̩] ), is a city in the Czech Republic. It is the fourth most populous city in the Czech Republic with about 188,000 inhabitants.

objects.title: Watch
objects.description: watch at the Metropolitan Museum of Art (MET, 17.101.7)

objects.title: Brooklyn Museum
objects.description: Art museum in Brooklyn, New York
objects.extract: The Brooklyn Museum is an art museum in the New York City borough of Brooklyn. At 560,000 square feet (52,000 m2), the museum is New York City's second largest and contains an art collection with around 500,000 objects.

objects.title: Kayak
objects.extract: A kayak is a small, narrow human-powered watercraft typically propelled by means of a long, double-bladed paddle. The word kayak originates from the Inuktitut word qajaq (IPA: [qajɑq]).

objects.title: Secret camera
objects.description: spy camera to wear under layers of clothes

objects.title: Bronze aryballos
objects.description: bottle at the Metropolitan Museum of Art (MET, 00.13.2)

objects.title: Mint (candy)
objects.extract: A mint or breath mint is a food item often consumed as an after-meal refreshment or before business and social engagements to improve breath odor. Mints are commonly believed to soothe the stomach, given their association with natural byproducts of the plant genus Mentha.

objects.title: Sari
objects.description: Woman's draped garment of the Indian subcontinent
objects.extract: A sari (Hindi: [saːɽiː]; sometimes also saree, sharee, or sadi) is a draped cloth and women's garment in the Indian subcontinent. It consists of an un-stitched stretch of woven fabric arranged over the body as a dress, with one end attached to the waist, while the other end rests over one shoulder as a stole, sometimes baring a part of the midriff.

objects.title: Sandwich
objects.description: Food made with bread and other ingredients
objects.extract: A sandwich is a dish typically consisting variously of meat, cheese, sauces, and vegetables used as a filling between slices of bread, or placed atop a slice of bread (known as an Open sandwich); or, more generally, any dish in which bread serves as a container or wrapper for another food type. The sandwich began as a portable, convenient food in the Western

objects.title: Grotesque (architecture)
objects.description: Fantastic or mythical figure used as architectural element
objects.extract: In architecture, a grotesque () is a fantastic or mythical figure carved from stone and fixed to the walls or roof of a building. A chimera () is a type of grotesque depicting a mythical combination of multiple animals (sometimes including humans).

objects.title: Durian
objects.extract: The durian ( ) is the edible fruit of several tree species belonging to the genus Durio. There are 30 recognised species, at least nine of which produce edible fruit.

objects.title: Lugana DOC
objects.description: Italian controlled designation of origin wine from Lombardy

objects.title: Dynamometer
objects.description: Machine used to measure force or mechanical power
objects.extract:
A dynamometer or "dyno" is a device for simultaneously measuring the torque and rotational speed (RPM) of an engine, motor or other rotating prime mover so that its instantaneous power may be calculated, and usually displayed by the dynamometer itself as kW or bhp.
In addition to being used to determine the torque or power characteristics of a machine under

objects.title: Upper Paleolithic
objects.description: Subdivision of the Paleolithic, or Old Stone Age
objects.extract: The Upper Paleolithic (or Upper Palaeolithic) is the third and last subdivision of the Paleolithic or Old Stone Age. Very broadly, it dates to between 50,000 and 12,000 years ago (the beginning of the Holocene), according to some theories coinciding with the appearance of behavioral modernity in humans.

objects.title: Light fixture
objects.description: Electrical device with an electric lamp
objects.extract: A light fixture (US English), light fitting (UK English) or luminaire is an electrical lighting device containing one or more light sources, such as light bulbs, and all the accessory components required for its operation to provide illumination to the environment. All light fixtures have a fixture body and one or more light sources.

objects.title: Dust mask
objects.description: Pad held over the nose and mouth to protect against dust
objects.extract: A dust mask is a flexible paper pad held over the nose and mouth made for protection against chronically toxic nuisance dusts, like from occupational exposure to plant dusts like hay. They are not intended to provide protection from most airborne hazards.

objects.title: Seiko
objects.extract: Seiko Group Corporation (セイコーグループ株式会社, Seikō Gurūpu kabushiki gaisha), commonly known as Seiko ( SAY-koh, Japanese: [seːkoː]), is a Japanese maker of watches, clocks, electronic devices, and semiconductors. Founded in 1881 by Kintarō Hattori in Tokyo, Seiko introduced the world's first commercial quartz wristwatch in 1969.

objects.title: Hot air balloon
objects.extract: A hot air balloon is a lighter-than-air aircraft consisting of a bag, called an envelope, which contains heated air. Suspended beneath is a gondola or wicker basket (in some long-distance or high-altitude balloons, a capsule), which carries passengers and a source of heat, in most cases an open flame caused by burning liquid propane.

objects.title: Lacquerware
objects.extract: Lacquerware are objects decoratively covered with lacquer. Lacquerware includes small or large containers, tableware, miscellaneous lacquered small objects carried by people, and larger objects such as furniture and even coffins painted with lacquer.

objects.title: Featherwork
objects.description: Use of feathers in traditional art
objects.extract: Featherwork is the working of feathers into a work of art or cultural artifact. Practiced in many parts of the world, this was especially elaborate among the peoples of Oceania and the Americas, such as the Incas and Aztecs.

objects.title: Syphilis
objects.extract: Syphilis () is a sexually transmitted infection caused by the bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pallidum. The signs and symptoms depend on the stage it presents: primary, secondary, latent or tertiary.

objects.title: Halberd of Christian I of Saxony (reigned 1586–91)
objects.description: halberd at the Metropolitan Museum of Art (MET, 04.3.80)

objects.title: Police car
objects.description: Ground vehicle used by police for transportation
objects.extract: A police car is an emergency vehicle used by police for transportation during patrols and responses to calls for service. Police cars are used by police officers to patrol a beat, quickly reach incident scenes, and transport and temporarily detain suspects.

objects.title: Inonotus hispidus
objects.description: Species of bracket fungus in the family Hymenochaetaceae
objects.extract: Inonotus hispidus, commonly known as shaggy bracket, is a North American fungus and plant pathogen.

objects.title: List of edible plants and mushrooms of southeast Alaska
objects.artist: Leslie Seaton from Seattle, WA, USA

objects.title: Combine harvester
objects.extract: The modern combine harvester, also called a combine, is a machine designed to harvest a variety of cultivated seeds. Combine harvesters are one of the most economically important labour-saving inventions, significantly reducing the fraction of the population engaged in agriculture.

objects.title: Chrysanthemum
objects.description: Genus of flowering plants in the daisy family
objects.extract: Chrysanthemums ( kriss-AN-thə-məmz), sometimes abbreviated to mums or chrysanths, are perennial herbaceous flowering plants in the family Asteraceae that bloom in the autumn. They are native to East Asia and northeastern Europe.

objects.title: Ginger beer
objects.extract: Ginger beer is a sweetened and carbonated, usually non-alcoholic, type of soft drink. Historically it was a type of beer brewed by the natural fermentation of prepared ginger spice, yeast and sugar.
objects.artist: Auckland Museum  Collections from Auckland, Aotearoa New Zealand

objects.title: Cockroach
objects.extract: Cockroaches (or roaches) are insects belonging to the order Blattodea (Blattaria). About 30 cockroach species out of 4,600 are associated with human habitats.

objects.title: Check (pattern)
objects.description: Pattern of intersecting vertical and horizontal stripes
objects.extract: Check (also checker, Brit: chequer, dicing, or checkerboard and checkerboard pattern) is a pattern of modified stripes consisting of crossed horizontal and vertical lines which form squares. The pattern typically contains two colours where a single checker (that is a single square within the check pattern) is surrounded on all four sides by a checker of a di

objects.title: Stained glass
objects.description: Colored glass and works that are made from it
objects.extract: Stained glass refers to coloured glass as a material or art and architectural works created from it. Although it is traditionally made in flat panels and used as windows, the creations of modern stained glass artists also include three-dimensional structures and sculpture.

objects.title: Lange (ski boots)
